question_html: Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac{\text {dx}}{\text {dy}}=\frac{\text e^{\frac {\text x}{\text y}}\left(\frac{\text x}{\text y}-1\right)}{1+\text e^{\frac {\text x}{\text y}}}{/tex}.
question_text: Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac{\text {dx}}{\text {dy}}=\frac{\text e^{\frac {\text x}{\text y}}\left(\frac{\text x}{\text y}-1\right)}{1+\text e^{\frac {\text x}{\text y}}}{/tex} .
explanation: Let x = vy {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dx}}{\text {dy}}{/tex} = v + y {tex}\frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dy}}{/tex} Substituting in the given differential equation, we get {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{y} \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dy}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text e^\text v(\mathrm{v}-1)}{\text e^\text v+1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{y} \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dy}}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{\left(\text e^\text v+\mathrm{v}\right)}{\text e^\text v+1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}+1}{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}+\mathrm{v}} \mathrm{dv}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{y}}{/tex} Integrating we get log |e v + v| = -log |y| + log C {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{e}^{\frac {\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}}+\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\mathrm{C}}{\mathrm{y}}{/tex} or {tex}\text y \mathrm{e}^{\frac {\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}}{/tex} + x = C ///////////////////
solution: Let x = vy {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dx}}{\text {dy}}{/tex} = v + y {tex}\frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dy}}{/tex} Substituting in the given differential equation, we get {tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{y} \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dy}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text e^\text v(\mathrm{v}-1)}{\text e^\text v+1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{y} \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dy}}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{\left(\text e^\text v+\mathrm{v}\right)}{\text e^\text v+1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}+1}{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}+\mathrm{v}} \mathrm{dv}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{y}}{/tex} Integrating we get log |e v + v| = -log |y| + log C {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{e}^{\frac {\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}}+\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\mathrm{C}}{\mathrm{y}}{/tex} or {tex}\text y \mathrm{e}^{\frac {\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}}{/tex} + x = C ///////////////////
solution_html:
Let x = vy {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dx}}{\text {dy}}{/tex} = v + y{tex}\frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dy}}{/tex}
Substituting in the given differential equation, we get
{tex}\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{y} \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dy}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text e^\text v(\mathrm{v}-1)}{\text e^\text v+1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{y} \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dy}}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{\left(\text e^\text v+\mathrm{v}\right)}{\text e^\text v+1}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}+1}{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{v}}+\mathrm{v}} \mathrm{dv}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{y}}{/tex}
Integrating we get
log |ev + v| = -log |y| + log C
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{e}^{\frac {\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}}+\frac{\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\mathrm{C}}{\mathrm{y}}{/tex} or {tex}\text y \mathrm{e}^{\frac {\mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{y}}}{/tex} + x = C
///////////////////

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}\frac {\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} + cot x{tex}\cdot{/tex}y = cos2 x, given that when x = {tex}\frac \pi 2{/tex}, y = 0.
question_text: Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}\frac {\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} + cot x {tex}\cdot{/tex} y = cos 2 x, given that when x = {tex}\frac \pi 2{/tex} , y = 0.
explanation: Integrating factor is {tex}\mathrm{e}^{\int \cot \text x \;\text {dx}}{/tex} = e log sin x = sin x Solution is y sin x = {tex}\int{/tex} cos 2 x sin x dx + C {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y sin x = {tex}-\frac{\cos ^3 \text x}{3}+\text C{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} , y = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} C = 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} particular solution is y sin x = {tex}-\frac{\cos ^3 \text x}{3}{/tex} or y = {tex}-\frac{\cos ^3 \text x}{3} \cdot \text {cosecx}{/tex} ////////////////////////
solution: Integrating factor is {tex}\mathrm{e}^{\int \cot \text x \;\text {dx}}{/tex} = e log sin x = sin x Solution is y sin x = {tex}\int{/tex} cos 2 x sin x dx + C {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y sin x = {tex}-\frac{\cos ^3 \text x}{3}+\text C{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} , y = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} C = 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} particular solution is y sin x = {tex}-\frac{\cos ^3 \text x}{3}{/tex} or y = {tex}-\frac{\cos ^3 \text x}{3} \cdot \text {cosecx}{/tex} ////////////////////////
solution_html:
Integrating factor is {tex}\mathrm{e}^{\int \cot \text x \;\text {dx}}{/tex} = elog sin x = sin x
Solution is y sin x = {tex}\int{/tex} cos2 x sin x dx + C
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y sin x = {tex}-\frac{\cos ^3 \text x}{3}+\text C{/tex}
x = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}, y = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} C = 0
{tex}\therefore{/tex} particular solution is y sin x = {tex}-\frac{\cos ^3 \text x}{3}{/tex} or y = {tex}-\frac{\cos ^3 \text x}{3} \cdot \text {cosecx}{/tex}
////////////////////////

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}- \frac {2\text y}{\text x}{/tex} = sin{tex}\frac {1}{\text x}{/tex}.
question_text: Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}- \frac {2\text y}{\text x}{/tex} = sin {tex}\frac {1}{\text x}{/tex} .
explanation: Integrating factor = {tex}\text e^{\int-\frac{2}{\text x} \text {dx}}{/tex} = e -2 log x = {tex}\frac{1}{\text x^2}{/tex} Solution is {tex}\mathrm{y} \cdot \frac{1}{\mathrm{x}^2}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\text x^2} \cdot \sin \frac{1}{\text x}{/tex} dx + C Let {tex}\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}{/tex} = t, {tex}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}^2}{/tex} dx = dt {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{\text x^2} \cdot \sin \frac{1}{\text x}{/tex} dx = {tex}-\int{/tex} sin t dt = cos t = cos {tex}\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\text y \cdot \frac{1}{\text x^2}{/tex} = cos {tex}\frac{1}{\text x}{/tex} + C ///////////////////////
solution: Integrating factor = {tex}\text e^{\int-\frac{2}{\text x} \text {dx}}{/tex} = e -2 log x = {tex}\frac{1}{\text x^2}{/tex} Solution is {tex}\mathrm{y} \cdot \frac{1}{\mathrm{x}^2}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\text x^2} \cdot \sin \frac{1}{\text x}{/tex} dx + C Let {tex}\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}{/tex} = t, {tex}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}^2}{/tex} dx = dt {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{\text x^2} \cdot \sin \frac{1}{\text x}{/tex} dx = {tex}-\int{/tex} sin t dt = cos t = cos {tex}\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\text y \cdot \frac{1}{\text x^2}{/tex} = cos {tex}\frac{1}{\text x}{/tex} + C ///////////////////////
solution_html:
Integrating factor = {tex}\text e^{\int-\frac{2}{\text x} \text {dx}}{/tex} = e-2 log x = {tex}\frac{1}{\text x^2}{/tex}
Solution is {tex}\mathrm{y} \cdot \frac{1}{\mathrm{x}^2}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\text x^2} \cdot \sin \frac{1}{\text x}{/tex}dx + C
Let {tex}\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}{/tex} = t, {tex}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}^2}{/tex}dx = dt
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{\text x^2} \cdot \sin \frac{1}{\text x}{/tex}dx = {tex}-\int{/tex}sin t dt = cos t = cos {tex}\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\text y \cdot \frac{1}{\text x^2}{/tex} = cos {tex}\frac{1}{\text x}{/tex} + C
///////////////////////

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find the particular solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac {\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = sin(x + y) + sin(x - y), given that when x = {tex}\frac {\pi}{4}{/tex}, y = 0.
question_text: Find the particular solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac {\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = sin(x + y) + sin(x - y), given that when x = {tex}\frac {\pi}{4}{/tex} , y = 0.
explanation: Given differential equation becomes {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = 2 sin x cos y {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sec y dy = 2 sin x dx Integrating we get log |sec y + tan y| = -2 cos x + C x = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} , y = 0 gives C = {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} particular solution is log |sec y + tan y| = -2 cos x + {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} ////////////////////
solution: Given differential equation becomes {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = 2 sin x cos y {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sec y dy = 2 sin x dx Integrating we get log |sec y + tan y| = -2 cos x + C x = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} , y = 0 gives C = {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} particular solution is log |sec y + tan y| = -2 cos x + {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} ////////////////////
solution_html:
Given differential equation becomes
{tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = 2 sin x cos y
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sec y dy = 2 sin x dx
Integrating we get
log |sec y + tan y| = -2 cos x + C
x = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}, y = 0 gives C = {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} particular solution is log |sec y + tan y| = -2 cos x + {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex}
////////////////////

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find the general solution of the differential equation: (xy - x2) dy = y2 dx.
question_text: Find the general solution of the differential equation: (xy - x 2 ) dy = y 2 dx.
explanation: Given differential equation can be written as {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text y^2}{\text {xy}-\text x^2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\left(\frac{\text y}{\text x}\right)^2}{\frac{\text y}{\text x}-1}{/tex} Put y = ux, {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = u + {tex}\text x\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} u + {tex}\text x\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text u^2}{\text u-1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\text x\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text u}{\text u-1}{/tex} Separating the variables and integrating {tex}\int\left(1-\frac{1}{\text u}\right) \text {du}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{\text {dx}}{\text x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} u - log u = log |x| + c {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}-\log \frac{\text y}{\text x}{/tex} = log |x| + c /////////////////
solution: Given differential equation can be written as {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text y^2}{\text {xy}-\text x^2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\left(\frac{\text y}{\text x}\right)^2}{\frac{\text y}{\text x}-1}{/tex} Put y = ux, {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = u + {tex}\text x\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} u + {tex}\text x\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text u^2}{\text u-1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\text x\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text u}{\text u-1}{/tex} Separating the variables and integrating {tex}\int\left(1-\frac{1}{\text u}\right) \text {du}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{\text {dx}}{\text x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} u - log u = log |x| + c {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}-\log \frac{\text y}{\text x}{/tex} = log |x| + c /////////////////
solution_html:
Given differential equation can be written as
{tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text y^2}{\text {xy}-\text x^2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\left(\frac{\text y}{\text x}\right)^2}{\frac{\text y}{\text x}-1}{/tex}
Put y = ux, {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = u + {tex}\text x\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} u + {tex}\text x\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text u^2}{\text u-1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\text x\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text u}{\text u-1}{/tex}
Separating the variables and integrating
{tex}\int\left(1-\frac{1}{\text u}\right) \text {du}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{\text {dx}}{\text x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} u - log u = log |x| + c {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}-\log \frac{\text y}{\text x}{/tex} = log |x| + c
/////////////////

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Find the general solution of the differential equation:
(x2 + 1) {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} + 2xy = {tex}\sqrt{\text x^2+4}{/tex}
question_text: Find the general solution of the differential equation: (x 2 + 1) {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} + 2xy = {tex}\sqrt{\text x^2+4}{/tex}
explanation: Rewriting the given differential equation as: {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}+\frac{2 \text x}{1+\text x^2} \text y{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{\text x^2+4}}{1+\text x^2}{/tex} Integrating factor = {tex}e^{\int \frac{2 \text x}{1+\text x^2} \text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\text e^{\log \left(1+\text x^2\right)}{/tex} = 1 + x 2 {tex}\therefore{/tex} solution of the differential equation is y(1 + x 2 ) = {tex}\int \frac{\sqrt{\text x^2+4}}{1+\text x^2} \cdot{/tex} (1 + x 2 )dx + c = {tex}\int \sqrt{\text x^2+4} \text {dx}+\text c{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} y(1 + x 2 ) = {tex}\frac{\text x \sqrt{\text x^2+4}}{2}{/tex} + 2 log {tex}\left|\text x+\sqrt{\text x^2+4}\right|{/tex} + c /////////////////////
solution: Rewriting the given differential equation as: {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}+\frac{2 \text x}{1+\text x^2} \text y{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{\text x^2+4}}{1+\text x^2}{/tex} Integrating factor = {tex}e^{\int \frac{2 \text x}{1+\text x^2} \text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\text e^{\log \left(1+\text x^2\right)}{/tex} = 1 + x 2 {tex}\therefore{/tex} solution of the differential equation is y(1 + x 2 ) = {tex}\int \frac{\sqrt{\text x^2+4}}{1+\text x^2} \cdot{/tex} (1 + x 2 )dx + c = {tex}\int \sqrt{\text x^2+4} \text {dx}+\text c{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} y(1 + x 2 ) = {tex}\frac{\text x \sqrt{\text x^2+4}}{2}{/tex} + 2 log {tex}\left|\text x+\sqrt{\text x^2+4}\right|{/tex} + c /////////////////////
solution_html:
Rewriting the given differential equation as:
{tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}+\frac{2 \text x}{1+\text x^2} \text y{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{\text x^2+4}}{1+\text x^2}{/tex}
Integrating factor = {tex}e^{\int \frac{2 \text x}{1+\text x^2} \text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\text e^{\log \left(1+\text x^2\right)}{/tex} = 1 + x2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} solution of the differential equation is
y(1 + x2) = {tex}\int \frac{\sqrt{\text x^2+4}}{1+\text x^2} \cdot{/tex}(1 + x2)dx + c
= {tex}\int \sqrt{\text x^2+4} \text {dx}+\text c{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} y(1 + x2) = {tex}\frac{\text x \sqrt{\text x^2+4}}{2}{/tex} + 2 log {tex}\left|\text x+\sqrt{\text x^2+4}\right|{/tex} + c
/////////////////////

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Find the general solution of the differential equation:
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex}(xy2) = 2y(1 + x2)
question_text: Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} (xy 2 ) = 2y(1 + x 2 )
explanation: Given differential equation is {tex}2 \mathrm{xy} \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}+\mathrm{y}^2{/tex} = 2y(1 + x 2 ) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}+\frac{\text y}{2\text x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\text x}+\mathrm{x}{/tex} Integrating factor = {tex}e^{\int \frac{1}{2 \text x} \text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}e^{\log \sqrt{x}}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{\text x}{/tex} Solution is given by {tex}\mathrm{y} \sqrt{x}{/tex} = {tex}\int\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{x}}+\mathrm{x}^{\frac{3}{2}}\right) \mathrm{dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{y} \sqrt{x}{/tex} = {tex}2 \sqrt{\text x}+\frac{2 \text x^{\frac{5}{2}}}{5}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} , or y = {tex}2+\frac{2 \text x^2}{5}+\frac{\text c}{\sqrt{\text x}}{/tex} ////////////////
solution: Given differential equation is {tex}2 \mathrm{xy} \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}+\mathrm{y}^2{/tex} = 2y(1 + x 2 ) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}+\frac{\text y}{2\text x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\text x}+\mathrm{x}{/tex} Integrating factor = {tex}e^{\int \frac{1}{2 \text x} \text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}e^{\log \sqrt{x}}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{\text x}{/tex} Solution is given by {tex}\mathrm{y} \sqrt{x}{/tex} = {tex}\int\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{x}}+\mathrm{x}^{\frac{3}{2}}\right) \mathrm{dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{y} \sqrt{x}{/tex} = {tex}2 \sqrt{\text x}+\frac{2 \text x^{\frac{5}{2}}}{5}+\mathrm{C}{/tex} , or y = {tex}2+\frac{2 \text x^2}{5}+\frac{\text c}{\sqrt{\text x}}{/tex} ////////////////
solution_html:
Given differential equation is
{tex}2 \mathrm{xy} \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}+\mathrm{y}^2{/tex} = 2y(1 + x2)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}+\frac{\text y}{2\text x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\text x}+\mathrm{x}{/tex}
Integrating factor = {tex}e^{\int \frac{1}{2 \text x} \text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}e^{\log \sqrt{x}}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{\text x}{/tex}
Solution is given by {tex}\mathrm{y} \sqrt{x}{/tex} = {tex}\int\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{x}}+\mathrm{x}^{\frac{3}{2}}\right) \mathrm{dx}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{y} \sqrt{x}{/tex} = {tex}2 \sqrt{\text x}+\frac{2 \text x^{\frac{5}{2}}}{5}+\mathrm{C}{/tex}, or y = {tex}2+\frac{2 \text x^2}{5}+\frac{\text c}{\sqrt{\text x}}{/tex}
////////////////

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Solve the following differential equation:
{tex}\text {xe}^{\frac{\text y}{\text x}}-\text y+\text x \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = 0
question_text: Solve the following differential equation: {tex}\text {xe}^{\frac{\text y}{\text x}}-\text y+\text x \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = 0
explanation: Given differential equation is {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}-\text e^{\frac{\text y}{\text x}}{/tex} Let y = vx {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = v + {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} The given equation becomes v + {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = v - e v {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -e -v dv = {tex}\frac{\text {dx}}{\text {x}}{/tex} Integrating both sides, we get e -v = log |x| + C {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}e^{-\frac{\text y}{\text x}}{/tex} = log |x| + C //////////////////////
solution: Given differential equation is {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}-\text e^{\frac{\text y}{\text x}}{/tex} Let y = vx {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = v + {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} The given equation becomes v + {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = v - e v {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -e -v dv = {tex}\frac{\text {dx}}{\text {x}}{/tex} Integrating both sides, we get e -v = log |x| + C {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}e^{-\frac{\text y}{\text x}}{/tex} = log |x| + C //////////////////////
solution_html:
Given differential equation is {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}-\text e^{\frac{\text y}{\text x}}{/tex}
Let y = vx {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = v + {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex}
The given equation becomes v + {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = v - ev
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -e-vdv = {tex}\frac{\text {dx}}{\text {x}}{/tex}
Integrating both sides, we get
e-v = log |x| + C
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}e^{-\frac{\text y}{\text x}}{/tex} = log |x| + C
//////////////////////

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}} = \frac{\text x+\text y}{\text x}{/tex}, y(1) = 0.
question_text: Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}} = \frac{\text x+\text y}{\text x}{/tex} , y(1) = 0.
explanation: {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text x+\text y}{\text x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = 1 + {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}{/tex} Let {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}{/tex} = v. Then x {tex}\frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} + v = {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} So, Differential equation becomes. {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}+\mathrm{v}{/tex} = 1 + v {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dv = {tex}\frac{\text {dx}}{\text x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} v = log |x| + c {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = x log |x| + cx {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 1, y = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} c = 0, y = x log |x| [can also be solved, taking first order linear diff. eqn] //////////////////////
solution: {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text x+\text y}{\text x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = 1 + {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}{/tex} Let {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}{/tex} = v. Then x {tex}\frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} + v = {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} So, Differential equation becomes. {tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}+\mathrm{v}{/tex} = 1 + v {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dv = {tex}\frac{\text {dx}}{\text x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} v = log |x| + c {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = x log |x| + cx {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 1, y = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} c = 0, y = x log |x| [can also be solved, taking first order linear diff. eqn] //////////////////////
solution_html:
{tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text x+\text y}{\text x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = 1 + {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}{/tex}
Let {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}{/tex} = v. Then x {tex}\frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} + v = {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex}
So, Differential equation becomes.
{tex}\mathrm{x} \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}+\mathrm{v}{/tex} = 1 + v
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dv = {tex}\frac{\text {dx}}{\text x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} v = log |x| + c
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = x log |x| + cx
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 1, y = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} c = 0, y = x log |x|
[can also be solved, taking first order linear diff. eqn]

question_html: Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{\text {xy}}{\text x^2+\text y^2}{/tex}, given that y = 1 when x = 0.
question_text: Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{\text {xy}}{\text x^2+\text y^2}{/tex} , given that y = 1 when x = 0.
explanation: {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{\text {xy}}{\text x^2+\text y^2}{/tex} ...(i) Put {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}{/tex} = v i.e. y = vx {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = v + {tex}\text x \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} Equation (i) gives v + {tex}\text x \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text v}{1+\text v^2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\text x \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{\text v^3}{1+\text v^2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1+\text v^2}{\text v^3} \text {dv}{/tex} = {tex}-\int \frac{\text {dx}}{\text x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{-1}{2 \text v^2}+\log |\text v|{/tex} = -log |x|+ log c putting v = {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}{/tex} and simplifying gives {tex}-\frac{\text x^2}{2 \text y^2}{/tex} = log {tex}\left|\frac{\text c}{\text y}\right|{/tex} now, x = 0, y = 1 gives c = 1 required solution is: {tex}\frac{\text x^2}{2 \text y^2}{/tex} = log |y|
solution: {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{\text {xy}}{\text x^2+\text y^2}{/tex} ...(i) Put {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}{/tex} = v i.e. y = vx {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = v + {tex}\text x \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} Equation (i) gives v + {tex}\text x \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text v}{1+\text v^2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\text x \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{\text v^3}{1+\text v^2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1+\text v^2}{\text v^3} \text {dv}{/tex} = {tex}-\int \frac{\text {dx}}{\text x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{-1}{2 \text v^2}+\log |\text v|{/tex} = -log |x|+ log c putting v = {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}{/tex} and simplifying gives {tex}-\frac{\text x^2}{2 \text y^2}{/tex} = log {tex}\left|\frac{\text c}{\text y}\right|{/tex} now, x = 0, y = 1 gives c = 1 required solution is: {tex}\frac{\text x^2}{2 \text y^2}{/tex} = log |y|
solution_html:
{tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}=\frac{\text {xy}}{\text x^2+\text y^2}{/tex} ...(i)
Put {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}{/tex} = v i.e. y = vx {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = v + {tex}\text x \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex}
Equation (i) gives v + {tex}\text x \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text v}{1+\text v^2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\text x \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{\text v^3}{1+\text v^2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1+\text v^2}{\text v^3} \text {dv}{/tex} = {tex}-\int \frac{\text {dx}}{\text x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{-1}{2 \text v^2}+\log |\text v|{/tex} = -log |x|+ log c
putting v = {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}{/tex} and simplifying gives
{tex}-\frac{\text x^2}{2 \text y^2}{/tex} = log {tex}\left|\frac{\text c}{\text y}\right|{/tex}
now, x = 0, y = 1 gives c = 1
required solution is: {tex}\frac{\text x^2}{2 \text y^2}{/tex} = log |y|

question_html: Find the particular solution of the differential equation (1 + x2) {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} + 2xy = {tex}\frac{1}{1+\text x^2}{/tex}, given that y = 0 when x = 1.
question_text: Find the particular solution of the differential equation (1 + x 2 ) {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} + 2xy = {tex}\frac{1}{1+\text x^2}{/tex} , given that y = 0 when x = 1.
explanation: Given diff.eqn.can be written as {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}+\frac{2 \text x}{1+\text x^2} \cdot \text y{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\left(1+\text x^2\right)^2}{/tex} I.F. = {tex}\text e^{\int \frac{2 \text x}{1+\text x^2} \text {dx}}{/tex} = 1 + x 2 solution is given by: y {tex}\cdot{/tex} (1 + x 2 ) = {tex}\int \frac{1}{1+\text x^2} \text {dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y {tex}\cdot{/tex} (1 + x 2 ) = tan -1 x + C Now x = 1, y = 0 gives C = {tex}-\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} Reqired solution: y {tex}\cdot{/tex} (1 + x 2 ) = tan -1 x {tex}-\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}
solution: Given diff.eqn.can be written as {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}+\frac{2 \text x}{1+\text x^2} \cdot \text y{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\left(1+\text x^2\right)^2}{/tex} I.F. = {tex}\text e^{\int \frac{2 \text x}{1+\text x^2} \text {dx}}{/tex} = 1 + x 2 solution is given by: y {tex}\cdot{/tex} (1 + x 2 ) = {tex}\int \frac{1}{1+\text x^2} \text {dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y {tex}\cdot{/tex} (1 + x 2 ) = tan -1 x + C Now x = 1, y = 0 gives C = {tex}-\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} Reqired solution: y {tex}\cdot{/tex} (1 + x 2 ) = tan -1 x {tex}-\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given diff.eqn.can be written as
{tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}+\frac{2 \text x}{1+\text x^2} \cdot \text y{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{\left(1+\text x^2\right)^2}{/tex}
I.F. = {tex}\text e^{\int \frac{2 \text x}{1+\text x^2} \text {dx}}{/tex} = 1 + x2
solution is given by: y{tex}\cdot{/tex}(1 + x2) = {tex}\int \frac{1}{1+\text x^2} \text {dx}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y{tex}\cdot{/tex}(1 + x2) = tan-1 x + C
Now x = 1, y = 0 gives C = {tex}-\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}
Reqired solution: y{tex}\cdot{/tex}(1 + x2) = tan-1 x{tex}-\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}

question_text: If y = (x + {tex}\sqrt {x^2 - 1}{/tex} ) 2 , then show that (x 2 - 1) {tex}\left(\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}\right)^2{/tex} = 4y 2 .
explanation: {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}2\left(\text x+\sqrt{\text x^2-1}\right)\left(1+\frac{\text x}{\sqrt{\text x^2-1}}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{2\left(\text x+\sqrt{\text x^2-1}\right)^2}{\sqrt{\text x^2-1}}{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{\text x^2-1} \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = 2y (x 2 - 1) {tex}\left(\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}\right)^2{/tex} = 4y 2
solution: {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}2\left(\text x+\sqrt{\text x^2-1}\right)\left(1+\frac{\text x}{\sqrt{\text x^2-1}}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{2\left(\text x+\sqrt{\text x^2-1}\right)^2}{\sqrt{\text x^2-1}}{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{\text x^2-1} \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = 2y (x 2 - 1) {tex}\left(\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}\right)^2{/tex} = 4y 2
solution_html:
{tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}2\left(\text x+\sqrt{\text x^2-1}\right)\left(1+\frac{\text x}{\sqrt{\text x^2-1}}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2\left(\text x+\sqrt{\text x^2-1}\right)^2}{\sqrt{\text x^2-1}}{/tex}
{tex}\sqrt{\text x^2-1} \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = 2y
(x2 - 1){tex}\left(\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}\right)^2{/tex} = 4y2

question_html: Solution of the differential equation (1 + y2) (1 + log x) dx + x dy = 0 is:
question_text: Solution of the differential equation (1 + y 2 ) (1 + log x) dx + x dy = 0 is:

options.explanation: {tex} \left(1+y^2\right)(1+\log x) d x+x d y=0 {/tex} {tex} x d y=-\left(1+y^2\right)(1+\log x) d x {/tex} {tex} \frac{x}{1+\log x} d y=-\left(1+y^2\right) d x {/tex} {tex} \frac{1}{1+y^2} d y=\frac{-(1+\log x)}{x} d x {/tex} vitegating both side {tex} \text { Mintegating both side } {/tex} {tex} \int \frac{1}{1+y^2} d y=-\int \frac{1+\log x}{x} d x {/tex} {tex} \tan ^{-1} y=-\int \frac{1+\log x}{x} d x {/tex} {tex} \text { nut } 1+\log x=t . {/tex} put {tex}1+\log x=t{/tex} . {tex} \frac{1}{x} d x=d t{/tex} {tex}\tan ^{-1} y=-\int \frac{\operatorname{tdt}}{}{/tex} {tex} \tan ^{-1} y=-\frac{t^2}{2}+c {/tex} {tex} 2 \tan ^{-1} y=-t^2+c {/tex} {tex} 2 \tan ^{-1} y=-(1+\log x)^2+c {/tex}

explanation: {tex} \left(1+y^2\right)(1+\log x) d x+x d y=0 {/tex} {tex} x d y=-\left(1+y^2\right)(1+\log x) d x {/tex} {tex} \frac{x}{1+\log x} d y=-\left(1+y^2\right) d x {/tex} {tex} \frac{1}{1+y^2} d y=\frac{-(1+\log x)}{x} d x {/tex} vitegating both side {tex} \text { Mintegating both side } {/tex} {tex} \int \frac{1}{1+y^2} d y=-\int \frac{1+\log x}{x} d x {/tex} {tex} \tan ^{-1} y=-\int \frac{1+\log x}{x} d x {/tex} {tex} \text { nut } 1+\log x=t . {/tex} put {tex}1+\log x=t{/tex} . {tex} \frac{1}{x} d x=d t{/tex} {tex}\tan ^{-1} y=-\int \frac{\operatorname{tdt}}{}{/tex} {tex} \tan ^{-1} y=-\frac{t^2}{2}+c {/tex} {tex} 2 \tan ^{-1} y=-t^2+c {/tex} {tex} 2 \tan ^{-1} y=-(1+\log x)^2+c {/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
An equation involving derivatives of the dependent variable with respect to the independent variables is called a differential equation. A differential equation of the form {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}{/tex} = F(x, y) is said to be homogeneous if F(x, y) is a homogeneous function of degree zero, whereas a function F(x, y) is a homogenous function of degree n if F{tex}(\lambda \text x, \lambda \mathrm{y}){/tex} = {tex}\lambda^{\mathrm{n}}{/tex} F(x, y). To solve a homogeneous differential equation of the type {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}{/tex} = F(x, y) = {tex}\mathrm{g}\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\text x}\right){/tex}, we make the substitution y = vx and then separate the variables.
Based on the above, answer the following questions:

  1. Show that (x2 - y2) dx + 2xy dy = 0 is a differential equation of the type {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex}= {tex}\mathrm{g}\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}\right){/tex}. (2)
  2. Solve the above equation to find its general solution. (2)
question_text: An equation involving derivatives of the dependent variable with respect to the independent variables is called a differential equation. A differential equation of the form {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}{/tex} = F(x, y) is said to be homogeneous if F(x, y) is a homogeneous function of degree zero, whereas a function F(x, y) is a homogenous function of degree n if F {tex}(\lambda \text x, \lambda \mathrm{y}){/tex} = {tex}\lambda^{\mathrm{n}}{/tex} F(x, y). To solve a homogeneous differential equation of the type {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}{/tex} = F(x, y) = {tex}\mathrm{g}\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\text x}\right){/tex} , we make the substitution y = vx and then separate the variables. Based on the above, answer the following questions: Show that (x 2 - y 2 ) dx + 2xy dy = 0 is a differential equation of the type {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\mathrm{g}\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}\right){/tex} . (2) Solve the above equation to find its general solution. (2)
explanation: (x 2 - y 2 )dx + 2xydy = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text y^2-\text x^2}{2 \text {xy}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\left(\frac{\text y}{\text x}\right)^2-1}{2\left(\frac{\text y}{\text x}\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\mathrm{g}\left(\frac{\text y}{\text x}\right){/tex} y = vx {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\text v+\text x \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} {tex}\text v+\text x \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text v^2-1}{2 \text v}-\text v{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1-\text v^2}{2 \text v}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{2 \text v}{1+\text v^2} \text {dv}{/tex} = {tex}-\int \frac{\mathrm{dx}}{\mathrm{x}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log |1 + v 2 | + log |x| = log C or {tex}\text x\left(1+\frac{\text y^2}{\text x^2}\right){/tex} = C or x 2 + y 2 = Cx
solution: (x 2 - y 2 )dx + 2xydy = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text y^2-\text x^2}{2 \text {xy}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\left(\frac{\text y}{\text x}\right)^2-1}{2\left(\frac{\text y}{\text x}\right)}{/tex} = {tex}\mathrm{g}\left(\frac{\text y}{\text x}\right){/tex} y = vx {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\text v+\text x \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} {tex}\text v+\text x \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text v^2-1}{2 \text v}-\text v{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1-\text v^2}{2 \text v}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{2 \text v}{1+\text v^2} \text {dv}{/tex} = {tex}-\int \frac{\mathrm{dx}}{\mathrm{x}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log |1 + v 2 | + log |x| = log C or {tex}\text x\left(1+\frac{\text y^2}{\text x^2}\right){/tex} = C or x 2 + y 2 = Cx
solution_html:
1. (x2 - y2)dx + 2xydy = 0
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text y^2-\text x^2}{2 \text {xy}}{/tex}
    = {tex}\frac{\left(\frac{\text y}{\text x}\right)^2-1}{2\left(\frac{\text y}{\text x}\right)}{/tex}
    = {tex}\mathrm{g}\left(\frac{\text y}{\text x}\right){/tex}
  2. y = vx {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\text v+\text x \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex}
    {tex}\text v+\text x \frac{\text {dv}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text v^2-1}{2 \text v}-\text v{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1-\text v^2}{2 \text v}{/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{2 \text v}{1+\text v^2} \text {dv}{/tex} = {tex}-\int \frac{\mathrm{dx}}{\mathrm{x}}{/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log |1 + v2| + log |x| = log C
    or {tex}\text x\left(1+\frac{\text y^2}{\text x^2}\right){/tex} = C
    or x2 + y2 = Cx

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Solve the following differential equation: {tex}\left(1+e^{\frac{y}{x}}\right) d y+e^{\frac{y}{x}}\left(1-\frac{y}{x}\right) dx{/tex} = 0
question_text: Solve the following differential equation: {tex}\left(1+e^{\frac{y}{x}}\right) d y+e^{\frac{y}{x}}\left(1-\frac{y}{x}\right) dx{/tex} = 0
explanation: {tex}\left(1+e^{\frac{y}{x}}\right) d y+e^{\frac{y}{x}}\left(1-\frac{y}{x}\right) dx=0{/tex} {tex}dy={\textstyle\frac{-e^\frac yx\left(1-\frac yx\right)dx}{\left(1+e^\frac yx\right)}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}={\textstyle\frac{e^\frac yx\left(\frac yx-1\right)}{\left(1+e^\frac yx\right)}}{/tex} As it is form of homogeneous equation Put y = vx, {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} {tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{e^\frac{vx}x\left(\frac{vx}x-1\right)}{\left(1+e^\frac{vx}x\right)}{/tex} {tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{e^v\left(v-1\right)}{\left(1+e^v\right)}{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{v.e^v-e^v}{\left(1+e^v\right)}-v{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{v.e^v-e^v-v-v.e^v}{\left(1+e^v\right)}{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{-\left(e^v+v\right)}{\left(1+e^v\right)}{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{1+e^v}{v+e^v}dv=-\int\frac{dx}x{/tex} {tex}\log\left|e^v+v\right|=-\log x+\log C{/tex} {tex}e^v+v=\frac Cx{/tex} {tex}e^{\textstyle\frac yx}+\frac yx=\frac Cx{/tex} {tex}xe^{\textstyle\frac yx}+y=C{/tex}
solution: {tex}\left(1+e^{\frac{y}{x}}\right) d y+e^{\frac{y}{x}}\left(1-\frac{y}{x}\right) dx=0{/tex} {tex}dy={\textstyle\frac{-e^\frac yx\left(1-\frac yx\right)dx}{\left(1+e^\frac yx\right)}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}={\textstyle\frac{e^\frac yx\left(\frac yx-1\right)}{\left(1+e^\frac yx\right)}}{/tex} As it is form of homogeneous equation Put y = vx, {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} {tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{e^\frac{vx}x\left(\frac{vx}x-1\right)}{\left(1+e^\frac{vx}x\right)}{/tex} {tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{e^v\left(v-1\right)}{\left(1+e^v\right)}{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{v.e^v-e^v}{\left(1+e^v\right)}-v{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{v.e^v-e^v-v-v.e^v}{\left(1+e^v\right)}{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{-\left(e^v+v\right)}{\left(1+e^v\right)}{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{1+e^v}{v+e^v}dv=-\int\frac{dx}x{/tex} {tex}\log\left|e^v+v\right|=-\log x+\log C{/tex} {tex}e^v+v=\frac Cx{/tex} {tex}e^{\textstyle\frac yx}+\frac yx=\frac Cx{/tex} {tex}xe^{\textstyle\frac yx}+y=C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\left(1+e^{\frac{y}{x}}\right) d y+e^{\frac{y}{x}}\left(1-\frac{y}{x}\right) dx=0{/tex}
{tex}dy={\textstyle\frac{-e^\frac yx\left(1-\frac yx\right)dx}{\left(1+e^\frac yx\right)}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}={\textstyle\frac{e^\frac yx\left(\frac yx-1\right)}{\left(1+e^\frac yx\right)}}{/tex}
As it is form of homogeneous equation
Put y = vx, {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex}
{tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{e^\frac{vx}x\left(\frac{vx}x-1\right)}{\left(1+e^\frac{vx}x\right)}{/tex}
{tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{e^v\left(v-1\right)}{\left(1+e^v\right)}{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{v.e^v-e^v}{\left(1+e^v\right)}-v{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{v.e^v-e^v-v-v.e^v}{\left(1+e^v\right)}{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{-\left(e^v+v\right)}{\left(1+e^v\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\int\frac{1+e^v}{v+e^v}dv=-\int\frac{dx}x{/tex}
{tex}\log\left|e^v+v\right|=-\log x+\log C{/tex}
{tex}e^v+v=\frac Cx{/tex}
{tex}e^{\textstyle\frac yx}+\frac yx=\frac Cx{/tex}
{tex}xe^{\textstyle\frac yx}+y=C{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find the general solution of the differential equation (x cos y)dy = ex(x log x + 1)dx.
question_text: Find the general solution of the differential equation (x cos y)dy = e x (x log x + 1)dx.
explanation: The given differential equation may be written as cos y dy = {tex}\left(e^{x} \log x+\frac{e^{x}}{x}\right) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \cos y \, d y=\int(\log x) e^{x} d x+\int \frac{e^{x}}{x} d x+C{/tex} where C is an arbitrary constant {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \sin y=(\log x) e^{x}-{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{x} \cdot e^{x} d x+\int \frac{e^{x}}{x} d x+C{/tex} [integrating by parts] {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \sin y=(\log x) e^{x}+C{/tex} this is the solution.
solution: The given differential equation may be written as cos y dy = {tex}\left(e^{x} \log x+\frac{e^{x}}{x}\right) d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \cos y \, d y=\int(\log x) e^{x} d x+\int \frac{e^{x}}{x} d x+C{/tex} where C is an arbitrary constant {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \sin y=(\log x) e^{x}-{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{x} \cdot e^{x} d x+\int \frac{e^{x}}{x} d x+C{/tex} [integrating by parts] {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \sin y=(\log x) e^{x}+C{/tex} this is the solution.
solution_html:
The given differential equation may be written as
cos y dy ={tex}\left(e^{x} \log x+\frac{e^{x}}{x}\right) d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \cos y \, d y=\int(\log x) e^{x} d x+\int \frac{e^{x}}{x} d x+C{/tex}
where C is an arbitrary constant
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \sin y=(\log x) e^{x}-{/tex}{tex}\int \frac{1}{x} \cdot e^{x} d x+\int \frac{e^{x}}{x} d x+C{/tex} [integrating by parts]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \sin y=(\log x) e^{x}+C{/tex} this is the solution.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find the general solution of the differential equation {tex}\log \left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)=(a x+b y){/tex}
question_text: Find the general solution of the differential equation {tex}\log \left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)=(a x+b y){/tex}
explanation: log {tex}\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)=a x+b y{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=e^{a x+b y}=e^{a x} \cdot e^{b y}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{e^{b y}} d y=e^{a x} d x{/tex} [on separating the variables] {tex}\Rightarrow \int e^{-b y} d y=\int e^{a x} d x{/tex} [integrating both sides] {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{e^{-b y}}{-b}=\frac{e^{a x}}{a}+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow a e^{-b y}+b e^{a x}=C^{\prime}{/tex} where C', = -ab Therefore {tex}a e^{-b y}+b e^{a x}=C^{\prime}{/tex} .is the required solution.
solution: log {tex}\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)=a x+b y{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=e^{a x+b y}=e^{a x} \cdot e^{b y}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{e^{b y}} d y=e^{a x} d x{/tex} [on separating the variables] {tex}\Rightarrow \int e^{-b y} d y=\int e^{a x} d x{/tex} [integrating both sides] {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{e^{-b y}}{-b}=\frac{e^{a x}}{a}+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow a e^{-b y}+b e^{a x}=C^{\prime}{/tex} where C', = -ab Therefore {tex}a e^{-b y}+b e^{a x}=C^{\prime}{/tex} .is the required solution.
solution_html:
log {tex}\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)=a x+b y{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=e^{a x+b y}=e^{a x} \cdot e^{b y}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{e^{b y}} d y=e^{a x} d x{/tex} [on separating the variables]
{tex}\Rightarrow \int e^{-b y} d y=\int e^{a x} d x{/tex}  [integrating both sides]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \frac{e^{-b y}}{-b}=\frac{e^{a x}}{a}+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow a e^{-b y}+b e^{a x}=C^{\prime}{/tex} where C', = -ab
Therefore {tex}a e^{-b y}+b e^{a x}=C^{\prime}{/tex}.is the required solution.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find the general solution of the differential equation sec2x tan y dx + sec2y tan x dy = 0
question_text: Find the general solution of the differential equation sec 2 x tan y dx + sec 2 y tan x dy = 0
explanation: Given Differential equation is, sec 2 x tan y dx + sec 2 y tan x dy = 0 Dividing by tan x tan y, we have {tex}\frac{{{{\sec }^2}x}}{{\tan x}}dx + \frac{{{{\sec }^2}y}}{{\tan y}}dy = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow\int\frac{{{{\sec }^2}x}}{{\tan x}}dx + \int\frac{{{{\sec }^2}y}}{{\tan y}}dy = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \log \left| {\tan x} \right| + \log \left| {\tan y} \right| = \log c{/tex} ... {tex}\left[ {\because \int {\frac{{f'\left( x \right)}}{{f\left( x \right)}}} dx = \log \left| {f\left( x \right)} \right|} \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \log \left| {\tan x\tan y} \right| = \log c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left| {\tan x\tan y} \right| = c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan x\tan y = \pm c = C{/tex} ... {tex}\left[ {\because \,\left| t \right| = a\left( {a \geqslant 0} \right) \Rightarrow t = \pm a} \right]{/tex}
solution: Given Differential equation is, sec 2 x tan y dx + sec 2 y tan x dy = 0 Dividing by tan x tan y, we have {tex}\frac{{{{\sec }^2}x}}{{\tan x}}dx + \frac{{{{\sec }^2}y}}{{\tan y}}dy = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow\int\frac{{{{\sec }^2}x}}{{\tan x}}dx + \int\frac{{{{\sec }^2}y}}{{\tan y}}dy = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \log \left| {\tan x} \right| + \log \left| {\tan y} \right| = \log c{/tex} ... {tex}\left[ {\because \int {\frac{{f'\left( x \right)}}{{f\left( x \right)}}} dx = \log \left| {f\left( x \right)} \right|} \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \log \left| {\tan x\tan y} \right| = \log c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left| {\tan x\tan y} \right| = c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan x\tan y = \pm c = C{/tex} ... {tex}\left[ {\because \,\left| t \right| = a\left( {a \geqslant 0} \right) \Rightarrow t = \pm a} \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Given Differential equation is, sec2x tan y dx + sec2y tan x dy = 0
Dividing by tan x tan y, we have
{tex}\frac{{{{\sec }^2}x}}{{\tan x}}dx + \frac{{{{\sec }^2}y}}{{\tan y}}dy = 0{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow\int\frac{{{{\sec }^2}x}}{{\tan x}}dx + \int\frac{{{{\sec }^2}y}}{{\tan y}}dy = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \log \left| {\tan x} \right| + \log \left| {\tan y} \right| = \log c{/tex}    ...{tex}\left[ {\because \int {\frac{{f'\left( x \right)}}{{f\left( x \right)}}} dx = \log \left| {f\left( x \right)} \right|} \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \log \left| {\tan x\tan y} \right| = \log c{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left| {\tan x\tan y} \right| = c{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \tan x\tan y = \pm c = C{/tex}    ... {tex}\left[ {\because \,\left| t \right| = a\left( {a \geqslant 0} \right) \Rightarrow t = \pm a} \right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find the general solution for differential equation:  {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x-y}+x^{2} e^{-y}{/tex}
question_text: Find the general solution for differential equation: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x-y}+x^{2} e^{-y}{/tex}
explanation: We have, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x} e^{-y}+x^{2} e^{-y}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=e^{-y}\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{e^{-y}}=\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right) d x{/tex} Integrating both the sides we get: {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{d y}{e^{-y}}=\int\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right) d x+c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow e^{y}=e^{x}+\frac{x^{3}}{3}+c{/tex} {tex}e^{y}=e^{x}+\frac{x^{2}}{3}+c{/tex}
solution: We have, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x} e^{-y}+x^{2} e^{-y}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=e^{-y}\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{e^{-y}}=\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right) d x{/tex} Integrating both the sides we get: {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{d y}{e^{-y}}=\int\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right) d x+c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow e^{y}=e^{x}+\frac{x^{3}}{3}+c{/tex} {tex}e^{y}=e^{x}+\frac{x^{2}}{3}+c{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x} e^{-y}+x^{2} e^{-y}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=e^{-y}\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{e^{-y}}=\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right) d x{/tex}
Integrating both the sides we get:
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{d y}{e^{-y}}=\int\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right) d x+c{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow e^{y}=e^{x}+\frac{x^{3}}{3}+c{/tex}
{tex}e^{y}=e^{x}+\frac{x^{2}}{3}+c{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Solve the following differential equation: (y-sin2x)dx + tan x dy = 0.
question_text: Solve the following differential equation: (y-sin 2 x)dx + tan x dy = 0.
explanation: {tex}\because{/tex} (y - sin 2 x) dx + tan x dy = 0 tan x dy = -(y - sin 2 x)dx {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(sin^2x-y)}{tanx}{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{sin^2x}{tanx}{/tex} - {tex}\frac{y}{tanx}{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx} + ycotx = sinx \space cosx{/tex} It is a differential equation in linear form {tex}\therefore{/tex} P = cotx, Q = sin x {tex}\cdot{/tex} cos x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I.F = {tex}e^{\int P d x}=e^{\int \cot x d x}{/tex} = {tex}e^{\log |\sin x|}=\sin x{/tex} So, solution is y {tex}\cdot{/tex} IF = {tex}\int Q \cdot I F d x+C{/tex} , y {tex}\cdot{/tex} sin x = sinx {tex}\cdot{/tex} cosx {tex}\cdot{/tex} sinx + c, y . sinx = {tex}\int \underbrace{\sin ^2 x \cdot \cos x}_I+c{/tex} ...(i) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int \sin ^2 x \cdot \cos x d x{/tex} Let sinx = t, cos x dx = dt {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\int t^2 d t=\frac{t^3}{3}+c_2{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\sin ^3 x}{3}+c_2{/tex} Put in eq n (i) y {tex}\cdot{/tex} sinx = {tex}\frac{\sin ^3 x}{3}+c_2+c_1{/tex} y {tex}\cdot{/tex} sinx = {tex}\frac{\sin ^3 x}{3}+c{/tex} Where (c = c 1 + c 2 )
solution: {tex}\because{/tex} (y - sin 2 x) dx + tan x dy = 0 tan x dy = -(y - sin 2 x)dx {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(sin^2x-y)}{tanx}{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{sin^2x}{tanx}{/tex} - {tex}\frac{y}{tanx}{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx} + ycotx = sinx \space cosx{/tex} It is a differential equation in linear form {tex}\therefore{/tex} P = cotx, Q = sin x {tex}\cdot{/tex} cos x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I.F = {tex}e^{\int P d x}=e^{\int \cot x d x}{/tex} = {tex}e^{\log |\sin x|}=\sin x{/tex} So, solution is y {tex}\cdot{/tex} IF = {tex}\int Q \cdot I F d x+C{/tex} , y {tex}\cdot{/tex} sin x = sinx {tex}\cdot{/tex} cosx {tex}\cdot{/tex} sinx + c, y . sinx = {tex}\int \underbrace{\sin ^2 x \cdot \cos x}_I+c{/tex} ...(i) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int \sin ^2 x \cdot \cos x d x{/tex} Let sinx = t, cos x dx = dt {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\int t^2 d t=\frac{t^3}{3}+c_2{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{\sin ^3 x}{3}+c_2{/tex} Put in eq n (i) y {tex}\cdot{/tex} sinx = {tex}\frac{\sin ^3 x}{3}+c_2+c_1{/tex} y {tex}\cdot{/tex} sinx = {tex}\frac{\sin ^3 x}{3}+c{/tex} Where (c = c 1 + c 2 )
solution_html:
{tex}\because{/tex} (y - sin2x) dx + tan x dy = 0
tan x dy = -(y - sin2x)dx
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(sin^2x-y)}{tanx}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{sin^2x}{tanx}{/tex} - {tex}\frac{y}{tanx}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx} + ycotx = sinx \space cosx{/tex}
It is a differential equation in linear form
{tex}\therefore{/tex} P = cotx, Q = sin x {tex}\cdot{/tex} cos x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I.F = {tex}e^{\int P d x}=e^{\int \cot x d x}{/tex} = {tex}e^{\log |\sin x|}=\sin x{/tex}
So, solution is
y {tex}\cdot{/tex} IF = {tex}\int Q \cdot I F d x+C{/tex},
y {tex}\cdot{/tex} sin x = sinx {tex}\cdot{/tex} cosx {tex}\cdot{/tex} sinx + c,
y . sinx = {tex}\int \underbrace{\sin ^2 x \cdot \cos x}_I+c{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int \sin ^2 x \cdot \cos x d x{/tex}
Let sinx = t, cos x dx = dt
{tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\int t^2 d t=\frac{t^3}{3}+c_2{/tex}
I = {tex}\frac{\sin ^3 x}{3}+c_2{/tex}
Put in eqn (i)
y {tex}\cdot{/tex} sinx = {tex}\frac{\sin ^3 x}{3}+c_2+c_1{/tex}
y {tex}\cdot{/tex} sinx = {tex}\frac{\sin ^3 x}{3}+c{/tex}
Where (c = c1 + c2)

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find the general solution of the differential equation: (x3 + y3)dy = x2ydx
question_text: Find the general solution of the differential equation: (x 3 + y 3 )dy = x 2 ydx
explanation: (x 3 + y 3 )dy = x 2 y dx {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{x^2y}{x^3+y^3}{/tex} It is homogeneous differential equation Put y = vx , {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} {tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{vx^3}{x^3+v^3x^3}{/tex} {tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac v{1+v^3}{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{v-v-v^4}{1+v^3}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1+v^3}{v^4}dv=-\frac{dx}x{/tex} {tex}\int\frac1{v^4}dv+\int\frac1vdv=-\int\frac{dx}x{/tex} {tex}-\frac1{3v^3}+\log\left|v\right|=-\log\left|x\right|+C{/tex} Put v = y/x {tex}-\frac{x^3}{3y^3}+\log\left|\frac yx\right|=-\log\left|x\right|+C{/tex} {tex}-\frac{x^3}{3y^3}+\log\left|y\right|=C{/tex} This is the required answer.
solution: (x 3 + y 3 )dy = x 2 y dx {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{x^2y}{x^3+y^3}{/tex} It is homogeneous differential equation Put y = vx , {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} {tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{vx^3}{x^3+v^3x^3}{/tex} {tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac v{1+v^3}{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{v-v-v^4}{1+v^3}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1+v^3}{v^4}dv=-\frac{dx}x{/tex} {tex}\int\frac1{v^4}dv+\int\frac1vdv=-\int\frac{dx}x{/tex} {tex}-\frac1{3v^3}+\log\left|v\right|=-\log\left|x\right|+C{/tex} Put v = y/x {tex}-\frac{x^3}{3y^3}+\log\left|\frac yx\right|=-\log\left|x\right|+C{/tex} {tex}-\frac{x^3}{3y^3}+\log\left|y\right|=C{/tex} This is the required answer.
solution_html:
(x3 + y3)dy = x2y dx
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{x^2y}{x^3+y^3}{/tex}
It is homogeneous differential equation
Put y = vx , {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex}
{tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{vx^3}{x^3+v^3x^3}{/tex}
{tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac v{1+v^3}{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{v-v-v^4}{1+v^3}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{1+v^3}{v^4}dv=-\frac{dx}x{/tex}
{tex}\int\frac1{v^4}dv+\int\frac1vdv=-\int\frac{dx}x{/tex}
{tex}-\frac1{3v^3}+\log\left|v\right|=-\log\left|x\right|+C{/tex}
Put v = y/x
{tex}-\frac{x^3}{3y^3}+\log\left|\frac yx\right|=-\log\left|x\right|+C{/tex}
{tex}-\frac{x^3}{3y^3}+\log\left|y\right|=C{/tex}
This is the required answer.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}\text{x} \frac{\text{dy}}{\text{dx}}{/tex} + 2y = x2 log x, given y(1) = 1.
question_text: Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}\text{x} \frac{\text{dy}}{\text{dx}}{/tex} + 2y = x 2 log x, given y(1) = 1.
explanation: x {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} +2y = x 2 log x {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} + {tex}\frac2x{/tex} .y = x log x It is form of {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} + Py = Q P = {tex}\frac2x{/tex} , Q = x log x I.F. = {tex}e^{\int pdx}=e^{\int\frac2xdx}=e^{2\log x}=e^{\log x^2}=x^2{/tex} So, general solution {tex}y.\left(I.F\right)=\int Q.(I.F)\;dx\;+\;C{/tex} {tex}y.x^2=\int x\;\log x.x^2\;dx\;+\;C{/tex} {tex}y.x^2=\int x^3\;\log x\;dx\;+\;C{/tex} {tex}y.x^2=\log x.\frac{x^4}4-\int\frac1x.\frac{x^4}4dx\;+\;C{/tex} {tex}y.x^2=\log x.\frac{x^4}4-\frac{x^4}{16}\;+\;C{/tex} {tex}y=\log x.\frac{x^2}4-\frac{x^2}{16}\;+\;\frac C{x^2}{/tex} As y(1) = 1 So, {tex}1=\log1.\frac{1^2}4-\frac{1^2}{16}\;+\;\frac C{1^2}{/tex} {tex}1=0-\frac1{16}\;+\;\frac C1{/tex} C = {tex}\frac{17}{16}{/tex} So particular solution {tex}y=\frac{x^2}4\log x-\frac{x^2}{16}+\frac{17}{16x^2}{/tex}
solution: x {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} +2y = x 2 log x {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} + {tex}\frac2x{/tex} .y = x log x It is form of {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} + Py = Q P = {tex}\frac2x{/tex} , Q = x log x I.F. = {tex}e^{\int pdx}=e^{\int\frac2xdx}=e^{2\log x}=e^{\log x^2}=x^2{/tex} So, general solution {tex}y.\left(I.F\right)=\int Q.(I.F)\;dx\;+\;C{/tex} {tex}y.x^2=\int x\;\log x.x^2\;dx\;+\;C{/tex} {tex}y.x^2=\int x^3\;\log x\;dx\;+\;C{/tex} {tex}y.x^2=\log x.\frac{x^4}4-\int\frac1x.\frac{x^4}4dx\;+\;C{/tex} {tex}y.x^2=\log x.\frac{x^4}4-\frac{x^4}{16}\;+\;C{/tex} {tex}y=\log x.\frac{x^2}4-\frac{x^2}{16}\;+\;\frac C{x^2}{/tex} As y(1) = 1 So, {tex}1=\log1.\frac{1^2}4-\frac{1^2}{16}\;+\;\frac C{1^2}{/tex} {tex}1=0-\frac1{16}\;+\;\frac C1{/tex} C = {tex}\frac{17}{16}{/tex} So particular solution {tex}y=\frac{x^2}4\log x-\frac{x^2}{16}+\frac{17}{16x^2}{/tex}
solution_html:
x {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} +2y = x2 log x
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} + {tex}\frac2x{/tex}.y = x log x
It is form of {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} + Py = Q
P = {tex}\frac2x{/tex}, Q = x log x
I.F. = {tex}e^{\int pdx}=e^{\int\frac2xdx}=e^{2\log x}=e^{\log x^2}=x^2{/tex}
So, general solution
{tex}y.\left(I.F\right)=\int Q.(I.F)\;dx\;+\;C{/tex}
{tex}y.x^2=\int x\;\log x.x^2\;dx\;+\;C{/tex}
{tex}y.x^2=\int x^3\;\log x\;dx\;+\;C{/tex}
{tex}y.x^2=\log x.\frac{x^4}4-\int\frac1x.\frac{x^4}4dx\;+\;C{/tex}
{tex}y.x^2=\log x.\frac{x^4}4-\frac{x^4}{16}\;+\;C{/tex}
{tex}y=\log x.\frac{x^2}4-\frac{x^2}{16}\;+\;\frac C{x^2}{/tex}
As y(1) = 1
So, {tex}1=\log1.\frac{1^2}4-\frac{1^2}{16}\;+\;\frac C{1^2}{/tex}
{tex}1=0-\frac1{16}\;+\;\frac C1{/tex}
C = {tex}\frac{17}{16}{/tex}
So particular solution
{tex}y=\frac{x^2}4\log x-\frac{x^2}{16}+\frac{17}{16x^2}{/tex}

And degree is highest power of highest derivative so degree is 2.

question_html: Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}+x \cos ^2\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{x}\right){/tex} = y; given that when x = 1, y = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}.
question_text: Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}+x \cos ^2\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{x}\right){/tex} = y; given that when x = 1, y = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}x\frac{dy}{dx}+x\cos^2\left(\frac yx\right)=y{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dy}{dx}=y-x\cos^2\left(\frac yx\right){/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac yx-\cos^2\left(\frac yx\right){/tex} ...(i) It is a homogeneous differential equation Put y = vx, {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} Putting in eqn. (i) {tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=v-\cos^2v{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=-\cos^2v{/tex} {tex}\frac{dv}{\cos^2v}=-\frac{dx}x{/tex} {tex}\int sec^2v\;dv=-\int\frac{dx}x{/tex} tan v = log x + C tan v - log x = C Put v = {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex} {tex}\tan\left(\frac yx\right)+\log x=C{/tex} ...(ii) Putting x = 1 and y = {tex}\frac {tex}\pi{/tex} 4{/tex} {tex}\tan\left(\frac\pi4\right)+0=C{/tex} C = 1 Putting in eqn (i) {tex}\tan\left(\frac yx\right)+\log x=1{/tex} This is the particular solution.
solution: {tex}x\frac{dy}{dx}+x\cos^2\left(\frac yx\right)=y{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dy}{dx}=y-x\cos^2\left(\frac yx\right){/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac yx-\cos^2\left(\frac yx\right){/tex} ...(i) It is a homogeneous differential equation Put y = vx, {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} Putting in eqn. (i) {tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=v-\cos^2v{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=-\cos^2v{/tex} {tex}\frac{dv}{\cos^2v}=-\frac{dx}x{/tex} {tex}\int sec^2v\;dv=-\int\frac{dx}x{/tex} tan v = log x + C tan v - log x = C Put v = {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex} {tex}\tan\left(\frac yx\right)+\log x=C{/tex} ...(ii) Putting x = 1 and y = {tex}\frac {tex}\pi{/tex} 4{/tex} {tex}\tan\left(\frac\pi4\right)+0=C{/tex} C = 1 Putting in eqn (i) {tex}\tan\left(\frac yx\right)+\log x=1{/tex} This is the particular solution.
solution_html:
{tex}x\frac{dy}{dx}+x\cos^2\left(\frac yx\right)=y{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{dy}{dx}=y-x\cos^2\left(\frac yx\right){/tex}
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac yx-\cos^2\left(\frac yx\right){/tex} ...(i)
It is a homogeneous differential equation
Put y = vx, {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex}
Putting in eqn. (i)
{tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=v-\cos^2v{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=-\cos^2v{/tex}
{tex}\frac{dv}{\cos^2v}=-\frac{dx}x{/tex}
{tex}\int sec^2v\;dv=-\int\frac{dx}x{/tex}
tan v = log x + C
tan v - log x = C
Put v = {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex}
{tex}\tan\left(\frac yx\right)+\log x=C{/tex} ...(ii)
Putting x = 1 and y = {tex}\frac{tex}\pi{/tex}4{/tex}
{tex}\tan\left(\frac\pi4\right)+0=C{/tex}
C = 1
Putting in eqn (i)
{tex}\tan\left(\frac yx\right)+\log x=1{/tex}
This is the particular solution.

question_html: Solve the differential equation: {tex}\log \left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right){/tex} = x - y.
question_text: Solve the differential equation: {tex}\log \left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right){/tex} = x - y.
explanation: {tex}\log\left(\frac{dy}{dx}\right)=\left(x-y\right){/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=e^{x-y}{/tex} ​ {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{e^x}{e^y}{/tex} {tex}\int e^ydy=\int e^xdx{/tex} {tex}e^y=e^x\;+C{/tex} {tex}e^y-e^x=C{/tex}
solution: {tex}\log\left(\frac{dy}{dx}\right)=\left(x-y\right){/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=e^{x-y}{/tex} ​ {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{e^x}{e^y}{/tex} {tex}\int e^ydy=\int e^xdx{/tex} {tex}e^y=e^x\;+C{/tex} {tex}e^y-e^x=C{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\log\left(\frac{dy}{dx}\right)=\left(x-y\right){/tex}
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=e^{x-y}{/tex}​
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{e^x}{e^y}{/tex}
{tex}\int e^ydy=\int e^xdx{/tex}
{tex}e^y=e^x\;+C{/tex}
{tex}e^y-e^x=C{/tex}

question_html: Solve the differential equation: {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}=\frac{x^2+y^2}{x y}{/tex}.
question_text: Solve the differential equation: {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}=\frac{x^2+y^2}{x y}{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{x^2+y^2}{xy}{/tex} is a homogeneous equation {tex}\therefore{/tex} put y = ux, {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=u+x\frac{du}{dx}{/tex} So, {tex}u+x\frac{du}{dx}=\frac{x^2+u^2x^2}{x\left(ux\right)}{/tex} {tex}u+x\frac{du}{dx}=\frac{x^2\left(1+u^2\right)}{x^2u}{/tex} {tex}x\frac{du}{dx}=\frac{\left(1+u^2\right)}u-u{/tex} {tex}x\frac{du}{dx}=\frac{\left(1+u^2\right)-u^2}u{/tex} {tex}\int udu=\int\frac{dx}x{/tex} {tex}\frac{u^2}2=\log x+C{/tex} Put u = {tex}\frac yx{/tex} {tex}\frac{y^2}{2x^2}=\log x+C{/tex} y 2 = 2x 2 logx + Cx 2
solution: {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{x^2+y^2}{xy}{/tex} is a homogeneous equation {tex}\therefore{/tex} put y = ux, {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=u+x\frac{du}{dx}{/tex} So, {tex}u+x\frac{du}{dx}=\frac{x^2+u^2x^2}{x\left(ux\right)}{/tex} {tex}u+x\frac{du}{dx}=\frac{x^2\left(1+u^2\right)}{x^2u}{/tex} {tex}x\frac{du}{dx}=\frac{\left(1+u^2\right)}u-u{/tex} {tex}x\frac{du}{dx}=\frac{\left(1+u^2\right)-u^2}u{/tex} {tex}\int udu=\int\frac{dx}x{/tex} {tex}\frac{u^2}2=\log x+C{/tex} Put u = {tex}\frac yx{/tex} {tex}\frac{y^2}{2x^2}=\log x+C{/tex} y 2 = 2x 2 logx + Cx 2
solution_html:
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{x^2+y^2}{xy}{/tex} is a homogeneous equation
{tex}\therefore{/tex} put y = ux, {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=u+x\frac{du}{dx}{/tex}
So, {tex}u+x\frac{du}{dx}=\frac{x^2+u^2x^2}{x\left(ux\right)}{/tex}
{tex}u+x\frac{du}{dx}=\frac{x^2\left(1+u^2\right)}{x^2u}{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{du}{dx}=\frac{\left(1+u^2\right)}u-u{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{du}{dx}=\frac{\left(1+u^2\right)-u^2}u{/tex}
{tex}\int udu=\int\frac{dx}x{/tex}
{tex}\frac{u^2}2=\log x+C{/tex}
Put u = {tex}\frac yx{/tex}
{tex}\frac{y^2}{2x^2}=\log x+C{/tex}
y2 = 2x2 logx + Cx2

question_html: Find the product of the order and the degree of the differential equation {tex}\left[\frac{d}{d x}\left(x y^2\right)\right] \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}+y{/tex} = 0.
question_text: Find the product of the order and the degree of the differential equation {tex}\left[\frac{d}{d x}\left(x y^2\right)\right] \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}+y{/tex} = 0.
explanation: {tex}\left[\frac d{dx}\left(xy^2\right)\right].\frac{dy}{dx}+y=0{/tex} {tex}\frac d{dx}\left(uv\right)=u\frac{dv}{dx}+v\frac{du}{dx}{/tex} So using {tex}\left(x\frac d{dx}y^2+y^2\frac{dx}{dx}\right)\frac{dy}{dx}+y=0{/tex} {tex}\left(2xy\frac{dy}{dx}+y^2\right)\frac{dy}{dx}+y=0{/tex} {tex}2xy\left(\frac{dy}{dx}\right)^2+y^2\frac{dy}{dx}+y=0{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} order = 1 Degree = 2 Product of order and degree = 1 {tex}\therefore{/tex} 2 = 2
solution: {tex}\left[\frac d{dx}\left(xy^2\right)\right].\frac{dy}{dx}+y=0{/tex} {tex}\frac d{dx}\left(uv\right)=u\frac{dv}{dx}+v\frac{du}{dx}{/tex} So using {tex}\left(x\frac d{dx}y^2+y^2\frac{dx}{dx}\right)\frac{dy}{dx}+y=0{/tex} {tex}\left(2xy\frac{dy}{dx}+y^2\right)\frac{dy}{dx}+y=0{/tex} {tex}2xy\left(\frac{dy}{dx}\right)^2+y^2\frac{dy}{dx}+y=0{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} order = 1 Degree = 2 Product of order and degree = 1 {tex}\therefore{/tex} 2 = 2
solution_html:
{tex}\left[\frac d{dx}\left(xy^2\right)\right].\frac{dy}{dx}+y=0{/tex}
{tex}\frac d{dx}\left(uv\right)=u\frac{dv}{dx}+v\frac{du}{dx}{/tex}
So using
{tex}\left(x\frac d{dx}y^2+y^2\frac{dx}{dx}\right)\frac{dy}{dx}+y=0{/tex}
{tex}\left(2xy\frac{dy}{dx}+y^2\right)\frac{dy}{dx}+y=0{/tex}
{tex}2xy\left(\frac{dy}{dx}\right)^2+y^2\frac{dy}{dx}+y=0{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} order = 1
Degree = 2
Product of order and degree = 1 {tex}\therefore{/tex} 2 = 2

question_html: Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}\left(y+3 x^2\right) \frac{d x}{d y}{/tex} = x, given that y = 1, when x = 1.
question_text: Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}\left(y+3 x^2\right) \frac{d x}{d y}{/tex} = x, given that y = 1, when x = 1.
explanation: {tex}\left(y+3 x^2\right) \frac{d x}{d y}=x{/tex} {tex}\frac{dx}{dy}=\frac x{\left(y+3x^2\right)}{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{y+3x^2}x{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac yx+3x{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}-\frac1xy=3x{/tex} It is in the form of a linear differential equation ∴ P = {tex}\frac{-1}x{/tex} , Q = 3x I.F. = {tex}e^{-\int\frac1xdx}=e^{-\log x}=e^{\log x^{-1}}=\frac1x{/tex} ∴ {tex}\frac yx=\int\frac1x.3x\;dx=3\int dx{/tex} {tex}\frac{y}x{/tex} = 3x + C y = 3x² + Cx ...(i) Now at x = 1, y = 1 So, 1 = 3 + C C = -2 Putting in eqn (i) y = 3x 2 - 2x
solution: {tex}\left(y+3 x^2\right) \frac{d x}{d y}=x{/tex} {tex}\frac{dx}{dy}=\frac x{\left(y+3x^2\right)}{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{y+3x^2}x{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac yx+3x{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}-\frac1xy=3x{/tex} It is in the form of a linear differential equation ∴ P = {tex}\frac{-1}x{/tex} , Q = 3x I.F. = {tex}e^{-\int\frac1xdx}=e^{-\log x}=e^{\log x^{-1}}=\frac1x{/tex} ∴ {tex}\frac yx=\int\frac1x.3x\;dx=3\int dx{/tex} {tex}\frac{y}x{/tex} = 3x + C y = 3x² + Cx ...(i) Now at x = 1, y = 1 So, 1 = 3 + C C = -2 Putting in eqn (i) y = 3x 2 - 2x
solution_html:
{tex}\left(y+3 x^2\right) \frac{d x}{d y}=x{/tex}
{tex}\frac{dx}{dy}=\frac x{\left(y+3x^2\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{y+3x^2}x{/tex}
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac yx+3x{/tex}
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}-\frac1xy=3x{/tex}
It is in the form of a linear differential equation
∴ P = {tex}\frac{-1}x{/tex}, Q = 3x
I.F. = {tex}e^{-\int\frac1xdx}=e^{-\log x}=e^{\log x^{-1}}=\frac1x{/tex}
∴ {tex}\frac yx=\int\frac1x.3x\;dx=3\int dx{/tex}
{tex}\frac{y}x{/tex} = 3x + C
y = 3x² + Cx ...(i)
Now at x = 1, y = 1
So, 1 = 3 + C
C = -2
Putting in eqn (i)
y = 3x2 - 2x

question_html: Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}\frac {dy} {dx}{/tex} = 2y2, given y = 1 when x = 1.
question_text: Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}\frac {dy} {dx}{/tex} = 2y 2 , given y = 1 when x = 1.
explanation: {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=2y^2{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{dy}{2y^2}=\int dx{/tex} {tex}\int dx=\frac12\int y^{-2}dy{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{-1}{2y}+C{/tex} ...(i) At y = 1, x = 1 So, {tex}1=\frac{-1}2+C{/tex} {tex}C=\frac32{/tex} Putting in eqn (i) {tex}x=\frac{-1}{2y}+\frac32{/tex} This is the particular solution.
solution: {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=2y^2{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{dy}{2y^2}=\int dx{/tex} {tex}\int dx=\frac12\int y^{-2}dy{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{-1}{2y}+C{/tex} ...(i) At y = 1, x = 1 So, {tex}1=\frac{-1}2+C{/tex} {tex}C=\frac32{/tex} Putting in eqn (i) {tex}x=\frac{-1}{2y}+\frac32{/tex} This is the particular solution.
solution_html:
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=2y^2{/tex}
{tex}\int\frac{dy}{2y^2}=\int dx{/tex}
{tex}\int dx=\frac12\int y^{-2}dy{/tex}
{tex}x=\frac{-1}{2y}+C{/tex} ...(i)
At y = 1, x = 1
So, {tex}1=\frac{-1}2+C{/tex}
{tex}C=\frac32{/tex}
Putting in eqn (i)
{tex}x=\frac{-1}{2y}+\frac32{/tex}
This is the particular solution.

question_html: Find the general solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}-y+x \sin \frac{y}{x}{/tex} = 0.
question_text: Find the general solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}-y+x \sin \frac{y}{x}{/tex} = 0.
explanation: x {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} - y + x sin {tex}(\frac{y}{x}){/tex} = 0 {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{y-xsin(\frac{d}{x})}{x}{/tex} ...(i) It is a homogenous differential equation put y = vx, & {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = v + x {tex}\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} putting in eq. (i) v + x {tex}\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{vx-xsin(\frac{vx}{v})}{x}{/tex} v + x {tex}\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} = v - sin v - {tex}\frac{dv}{sinv}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{dx}{x}{/tex} {tex}\int \operatorname{cosec} v\ d v=-\int \frac{d x}{x}{/tex} log {tex}|\operatorname{cosec} \theta-\cot v|{/tex} = - log x + log c log {tex}|\operatorname{cosec} \theta-\cot v|{/tex} = log {tex}(\frac{c}{x}){/tex} cosec v - cot v = {tex}\frac{c}{x}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{sinv}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{cas v}{sinv}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{c}{x}{/tex} 1 - cos v = {tex}\frac{c}{x}{/tex} sin v x[1 - cos {tex}(\frac{y}{x}){/tex} ] = c sin {tex}(\frac{y}{x}){/tex}
solution: x {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} - y + x sin {tex}(\frac{y}{x}){/tex} = 0 {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{y-xsin(\frac{d}{x})}{x}{/tex} ...(i) It is a homogenous differential equation put y = vx, & {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = v + x {tex}\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} putting in eq. (i) v + x {tex}\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{vx-xsin(\frac{vx}{v})}{x}{/tex} v + x {tex}\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} = v - sin v - {tex}\frac{dv}{sinv}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{dx}{x}{/tex} {tex}\int \operatorname{cosec} v\ d v=-\int \frac{d x}{x}{/tex} log {tex}|\operatorname{cosec} \theta-\cot v|{/tex} = - log x + log c log {tex}|\operatorname{cosec} \theta-\cot v|{/tex} = log {tex}(\frac{c}{x}){/tex} cosec v - cot v = {tex}\frac{c}{x}{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{sinv}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{cas v}{sinv}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{c}{x}{/tex} 1 - cos v = {tex}\frac{c}{x}{/tex} sin v x[1 - cos {tex}(\frac{y}{x}){/tex} ] = c sin {tex}(\frac{y}{x}){/tex}
solution_html:
x {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} - y + x sin {tex}(\frac{y}{x}){/tex} = 0
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{y-xsin(\frac{d}{x})}{x}{/tex} ...(i)
It is a homogenous differential equation
put y = vx, & {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = v + x{tex}\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex}
putting in eq. (i)
v + x{tex}\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{vx-xsin(\frac{vx}{v})}{x}{/tex}
v + x{tex}\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} = v - sin v
-{tex}\frac{dv}{sinv}{/tex}= {tex}\frac{dx}{x}{/tex}
{tex}\int \operatorname{cosec} v\ d v=-\int \frac{d x}{x}{/tex}
log{tex}|\operatorname{cosec} \theta-\cot v|{/tex} = - log x + log c
log {tex}|\operatorname{cosec} \theta-\cot v|{/tex} = log{tex}(\frac{c}{x}){/tex}
cosec v - cot v = {tex}\frac{c}{x}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{1}{sinv}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{cas v}{sinv}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{c}{x}{/tex}
1 - cos v = {tex}\frac{c}{x}{/tex} sin v
x[1 - cos{tex}(\frac{y}{x}){/tex}] = c sin{tex}(\frac{y}{x}){/tex}

question_html: Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}-\mathrm{y}{/tex} = {tex}x^2 \cdot \mathrm{e}^x{/tex}, given y(1) = 0.
question_text: Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}-\mathrm{y}{/tex} = {tex}x^2 \cdot \mathrm{e}^x{/tex} , given y(1) = 0.
explanation: {tex}x\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}-\mathrm y=\mathrm x^2.\mathrm e^{\mathrm x}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}-\frac1x\mathrm y=\mathrm x.\mathrm e^{\mathrm x}{/tex} It is form of linear differential equation So, P = {tex}\frac{-1}x{/tex} , {tex}Q=x.e^x{/tex} IF= {tex}e^{\int Pdx}=e^{-\int\frac1xdx}=e^{-\log x}=e^{\log x^{-1}}=\frac1x{/tex} General solution y.(IF) = {tex}\int Q\left(IF\right)dx+C{/tex} {tex}y.\frac1x=\int x.e^x.\frac1xdx+C{/tex} {tex}\frac yx=e^x+C{/tex} y = x e x + Cx ...(i) Put x = 1, and y = 0 0 = e + C C = e Putting in eqn (i) y = xe x + xe​​​​​​
solution: {tex}x\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}-\mathrm y=\mathrm x^2.\mathrm e^{\mathrm x}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}-\frac1x\mathrm y=\mathrm x.\mathrm e^{\mathrm x}{/tex} It is form of linear differential equation So, P = {tex}\frac{-1}x{/tex} , {tex}Q=x.e^x{/tex} IF= {tex}e^{\int Pdx}=e^{-\int\frac1xdx}=e^{-\log x}=e^{\log x^{-1}}=\frac1x{/tex} General solution y.(IF) = {tex}\int Q\left(IF\right)dx+C{/tex} {tex}y.\frac1x=\int x.e^x.\frac1xdx+C{/tex} {tex}\frac yx=e^x+C{/tex} y = x e x + Cx ...(i) Put x = 1, and y = 0 0 = e + C C = e Putting in eqn (i) y = xe x + xe​​​​​​
solution_html:
{tex}x\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}-\mathrm y=\mathrm x^2.\mathrm e^{\mathrm x}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}-\frac1x\mathrm y=\mathrm x.\mathrm e^{\mathrm x}{/tex}
It is form of linear differential equation
So, P = {tex}\frac{-1}x{/tex} , {tex}Q=x.e^x{/tex}
IF= {tex}e^{\int Pdx}=e^{-\int\frac1xdx}=e^{-\log x}=e^{\log x^{-1}}=\frac1x{/tex}
General solution
y.(IF) = {tex}\int Q\left(IF\right)dx+C{/tex}
{tex}y.\frac1x=\int x.e^x.\frac1xdx+C{/tex}
{tex}\frac yx=e^x+C{/tex}
y = xex + Cx ...(i)
Put x = 1, and y = 0
0 = e + C
C = e
Putting in eqn (i)
y = xex + xe​​​​​​

question_html: Find the general solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}{/tex} = y(log y - log x + 1).
question_text: Find the general solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}{/tex} = y(log y - log x + 1).
explanation: {tex}x\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = y(log y - log x + 1) {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac yx\left(\log\left({\textstyle\frac yx}\right)+1\right){/tex} ( {tex}\because{/tex} log a - log b = loga/b) Put y = vx , {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} {tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=v\left(\log\;v+1\right){/tex} {tex}\frac{dv}{v\;\log v}=\frac{dx}x{/tex} Integrating both sides log (logv) = log x + log C log (logv) = logxC log v = Cx {tex}\log\frac yx{/tex} = Cx
solution: {tex}x\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = y(log y - log x + 1) {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac yx\left(\log\left({\textstyle\frac yx}\right)+1\right){/tex} ( {tex}\because{/tex} log a - log b = loga/b) Put y = vx , {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} {tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=v\left(\log\;v+1\right){/tex} {tex}\frac{dv}{v\;\log v}=\frac{dx}x{/tex} Integrating both sides log (logv) = log x + log C log (logv) = logxC log v = Cx {tex}\log\frac yx{/tex} = Cx
solution_html:
{tex}x\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = y(log y - log x + 1)
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac yx\left(\log\left({\textstyle\frac yx}\right)+1\right){/tex} ({tex}\because{/tex} log a - log b = loga/b)
Put y = vx , {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex}
{tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=v\left(\log\;v+1\right){/tex}
{tex}\frac{dv}{v\;\log v}=\frac{dx}x{/tex}
Integrating both sides
log (logv) = log x + log C
log (logv) = logxC
log v = Cx
{tex}\log\frac yx{/tex} = Cx

question_html: Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}=\frac{3 \mathrm{e}^{2 x}+3 \mathrm{e}^{4 x}}{\mathrm{e}^x+\mathrm{e}^{-x}}{/tex}
question_text: Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}=\frac{3 \mathrm{e}^{2 x}+3 \mathrm{e}^{4 x}}{\mathrm{e}^x+\mathrm{e}^{-x}}{/tex}
explanation: {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}=\frac{3 \mathrm{e}^{2 x}+3 \mathrm{e}^{4 x}}{\mathrm{e}^x+\mathrm{e}^{-x}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=\frac{3\mathrm e^{2x}+3\mathrm e^{4x}}{\mathrm e^x+\frac1{e^x}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=\frac{\left(3\mathrm e^{2\mathrm x}+3\mathrm e^{4\mathrm x}\right).e^x}{\mathrm e^{2x}+1}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=\frac{3e^{2x}\left(1+\mathrm e^{2\mathrm x}\right).e^x}{\mathrm e^{2x}+1}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=3e^{3x}{/tex} {tex}\int dy=\int\left(3e^{3x}\right)dx{/tex} (integrating both sides) {tex}y=3{\textstyle\frac{e^{3x}}3}+C{/tex} y = e 3x + C This is the general solution.
solution: {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}=\frac{3 \mathrm{e}^{2 x}+3 \mathrm{e}^{4 x}}{\mathrm{e}^x+\mathrm{e}^{-x}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=\frac{3\mathrm e^{2x}+3\mathrm e^{4x}}{\mathrm e^x+\frac1{e^x}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=\frac{\left(3\mathrm e^{2\mathrm x}+3\mathrm e^{4\mathrm x}\right).e^x}{\mathrm e^{2x}+1}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=\frac{3e^{2x}\left(1+\mathrm e^{2\mathrm x}\right).e^x}{\mathrm e^{2x}+1}{/tex} {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=3e^{3x}{/tex} {tex}\int dy=\int\left(3e^{3x}\right)dx{/tex} (integrating both sides) {tex}y=3{\textstyle\frac{e^{3x}}3}+C{/tex} y = e 3x + C This is the general solution.
solution_html:
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}=\frac{3 \mathrm{e}^{2 x}+3 \mathrm{e}^{4 x}}{\mathrm{e}^x+\mathrm{e}^{-x}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=\frac{3\mathrm e^{2x}+3\mathrm e^{4x}}{\mathrm e^x+\frac1{e^x}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=\frac{\left(3\mathrm e^{2\mathrm x}+3\mathrm e^{4\mathrm x}\right).e^x}{\mathrm e^{2x}+1}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=\frac{3e^{2x}\left(1+\mathrm e^{2\mathrm x}\right).e^x}{\mathrm e^{2x}+1}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm dx}=3e^{3x}{/tex}
{tex}\int dy=\int\left(3e^{3x}\right)dx{/tex} (integrating both sides)
{tex}y=3{\textstyle\frac{e^{3x}}3}+C{/tex}
y = e3x + C
This is the general solution.

question_html: Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}+y+\frac{1}{1+x^2}{/tex} = 0, given that y(1) = 0.
question_text: Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}+y+\frac{1}{1+x^2}{/tex} = 0, given that y(1) = 0.
explanation: {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}+y+\frac{1}{1+x^2}=0{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dy}{dx}+y=-\frac1{1+x^2}{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}+\left(\frac1x\right)y=-\frac1{x\left(1+x^2\right)}{/tex} So it a linear differential equation P = {tex}\frac1x{/tex} and Q = {tex}-\frac1{x\left(1+x^2\right)}{/tex} {tex}IF=e^{\int pdx}=e^{\int{\textstyle\frac1x}dx}=e^{\log x}=\;x{/tex} General solution {tex}y.IF=\int Q.IFdx+C{/tex} {tex}y.x=-\int\frac1{x\left(1+x^2\right)}.x\;dx+C{/tex} {tex}y.x=-\int\frac1{\left(1+x^2\right)}\;dx+C{/tex} {tex}y.x=-\tan^{-1}x+C{/tex} ...(i) At x =1, y = 0 {tex}0=-\tan^{-1}\left(1\right)+C{/tex} {tex}C=\frac\pi4{/tex} Putting in eqn (i) {tex}y.x=-\tan^{-1}x+\frac\pi4{/tex}
solution: {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}+y+\frac{1}{1+x^2}=0{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dy}{dx}+y=-\frac1{1+x^2}{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}+\left(\frac1x\right)y=-\frac1{x\left(1+x^2\right)}{/tex} So it a linear differential equation P = {tex}\frac1x{/tex} and Q = {tex}-\frac1{x\left(1+x^2\right)}{/tex} {tex}IF=e^{\int pdx}=e^{\int{\textstyle\frac1x}dx}=e^{\log x}=\;x{/tex} General solution {tex}y.IF=\int Q.IFdx+C{/tex} {tex}y.x=-\int\frac1{x\left(1+x^2\right)}.x\;dx+C{/tex} {tex}y.x=-\int\frac1{\left(1+x^2\right)}\;dx+C{/tex} {tex}y.x=-\tan^{-1}x+C{/tex} ...(i) At x =1, y = 0 {tex}0=-\tan^{-1}\left(1\right)+C{/tex} {tex}C=\frac\pi4{/tex} Putting in eqn (i) {tex}y.x=-\tan^{-1}x+\frac\pi4{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}+y+\frac{1}{1+x^2}=0{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{dy}{dx}+y=-\frac1{1+x^2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}+\left(\frac1x\right)y=-\frac1{x\left(1+x^2\right)}{/tex}
So it a linear differential equation
P = {tex}\frac1x{/tex} and Q = {tex}-\frac1{x\left(1+x^2\right)}{/tex}
{tex}IF=e^{\int pdx}=e^{\int{\textstyle\frac1x}dx}=e^{\log x}=\;x{/tex}
General solution
{tex}y.IF=\int Q.IFdx+C{/tex}
{tex}y.x=-\int\frac1{x\left(1+x^2\right)}.x\;dx+C{/tex}
{tex}y.x=-\int\frac1{\left(1+x^2\right)}\;dx+C{/tex}
{tex}y.x=-\tan^{-1}x+C{/tex} ...(i)
At x =1, y = 0
{tex}0=-\tan^{-1}\left(1\right)+C{/tex}
{tex}C=\frac\pi4{/tex}
Putting in eqn (i)
{tex}y.x=-\tan^{-1}x+\frac\pi4{/tex}

question_html: Find the general solution of the differential equation x (y3 + x3) dy = (2y4 + 5x3y) dx.
question_text: Find the general solution of the differential equation x (y 3 + x 3 ) dy = (2y 4 + 5x 3 y) dx.
explanation: x(y 3 + x 3 ) dy = (2y 4 + 5x 3 y) dx {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{2y^4+5x^3y}{xy^3+x^4}{/tex} It is a homogeneous differential equation So put y = vx, {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} {tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{2v^4+5v}{v^3+1}{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{2v^4+5v}{v^3+1}-v{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{2v^4+5v-v^4-v}{v^3+1}{/tex} {tex}\frac{v^3+1}{v^4+4v}dv=\frac{dx}x{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{4v^3+4}{v^4+4v}dv=4\int\frac{dx}x{/tex} log|v 4 + 4v| = 4log x + log C log|v 4 + 4v| = log(x) 4 + log C log|v 4 + 4v| = log Cx 4 {tex}{/tex} v 4 + 4v = Cx 4 Put v = {tex}\frac yx{/tex} {tex}\frac{y^4}{x^4}+4\frac yx{/tex} = Cx 4 y 4 + 4yx 3 = Cx 8
solution: x(y 3 + x 3 ) dy = (2y 4 + 5x 3 y) dx {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{2y^4+5x^3y}{xy^3+x^4}{/tex} It is a homogeneous differential equation So put y = vx, {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} {tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{2v^4+5v}{v^3+1}{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{2v^4+5v}{v^3+1}-v{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{2v^4+5v-v^4-v}{v^3+1}{/tex} {tex}\frac{v^3+1}{v^4+4v}dv=\frac{dx}x{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{4v^3+4}{v^4+4v}dv=4\int\frac{dx}x{/tex} log|v 4 + 4v| = 4log x + log C log|v 4 + 4v| = log(x) 4 + log C log|v 4 + 4v| = log Cx 4 {tex}{/tex} v 4 + 4v = Cx 4 Put v = {tex}\frac yx{/tex} {tex}\frac{y^4}{x^4}+4\frac yx{/tex} = Cx 4 y 4 + 4yx 3 = Cx 8
solution_html:
x(y3 + x3) dy = (2y4 + 5x3y) dx
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{2y^4+5x^3y}{xy^3+x^4}{/tex}
It is a homogeneous differential equation
So put y = vx, {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex}
{tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{2v^4+5v}{v^3+1}{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{2v^4+5v}{v^3+1}-v{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{2v^4+5v-v^4-v}{v^3+1}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{v^3+1}{v^4+4v}dv=\frac{dx}x{/tex}
{tex}\int\frac{4v^3+4}{v^4+4v}dv=4\int\frac{dx}x{/tex}
log|v4 + 4v| = 4log x + log C
log|v4 + 4v| = log(x)4 + log C
log|v4 + 4v| = log Cx4
{tex}{/tex}v4 + 4v = Cx4
Put v = {tex}\frac yx{/tex}
{tex}\frac{y^4}{x^4}+4\frac yx{/tex} = Cx4
y4 + 4yx3 = Cx8
