question_html: Verify that y = a cos (log x) + b sin (log x) is a solution of the differential equation {tex}x^{2}+\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}+x \frac{d y}{d x}+y{/tex} = 0
question_text: Verify that y = a cos (log x) + b sin (log x) is a solution of the differential equation {tex}x^{2}+\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}+x \frac{d y}{d x}+y{/tex} = 0
explanation: Given: y = a cos (log x) + b sin (log x) ...(i) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-a \sin (\log x)}{x}+\frac{b \cos (\log x)}{x}{/tex} [on differentiating (i) w.r.t.x] {tex}\Rightarrow \quad x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = -a sin (log x) + b cos (log x) ...(ii) {tex}\Rightarrow \quad x \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}+\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-a \cos (\log x)}{x}-\frac{b \sin (\log x)}{x}{/tex} [on differentiating (ii) w.r.t. x] {tex}\Rightarrow \quad x^{2} \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}+x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = -a cos(log x) - b sin (log x) {tex}\Rightarrow \quad x^{2} \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}+x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} + y = 0 [using (i)] Hence, y = a cos (log x) + b sin (log x) is a solution of differential equation {tex}x^{2} \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}+x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} + y = 0.
solution: Given: y = a cos (log x) + b sin (log x) ...(i) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-a \sin (\log x)}{x}+\frac{b \cos (\log x)}{x}{/tex} [on differentiating (i) w.r.t.x] {tex}\Rightarrow \quad x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = -a sin (log x) + b cos (log x) ...(ii) {tex}\Rightarrow \quad x \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}+\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-a \cos (\log x)}{x}-\frac{b \sin (\log x)}{x}{/tex} [on differentiating (ii) w.r.t. x] {tex}\Rightarrow \quad x^{2} \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}+x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = -a cos(log x) - b sin (log x) {tex}\Rightarrow \quad x^{2} \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}+x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} + y = 0 [using (i)] Hence, y = a cos (log x) + b sin (log x) is a solution of differential equation {tex}x^{2} \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}+x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} + y = 0.
solution_html:
Given: y = a cos (log x) + b sin (log x) ...(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-a \sin (\log x)}{x}+\frac{b \cos (\log x)}{x}{/tex} [on differentiating (i) w.r.t.x]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = -a sin (log x) + b cos (log x) ...(ii)
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}+\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-a \cos (\log x)}{x}-\frac{b \sin (\log x)}{x}{/tex} [on differentiating (ii) w.r.t. x]
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x^{2} \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}+x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = -a cos(log x) - b sin (log x)
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x^{2} \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}+x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} + y = 0 [using (i)]
Hence, y = a cos (log x) + b sin (log x) is a solution of differential equation {tex}x^{2} \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}+x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} + y = 0.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+2 y=6 e^{x}{/tex}
question_text: Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+2 y=6 e^{x}{/tex}
explanation: The given differential equation is:- {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+2 y=6 e^{x}{/tex} This is of the form {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex} Where P = 2 and Q = 6.e x Thus the given differential equation is linear differential equation Now, {tex}I F=e^{\int P dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I F=e^{\int 2 \, d x} \Rightarrow I F=e^{2 x}{/tex} Solution is {tex}y \cdot(I F)=\int(I F) Q \, d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot e^{2 x}=\int e^{2 x} \cdot 6 \cdot e^{x} d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot e^{2 x}=6 \int e^{3 x} d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot e^{2 x}=\frac{6}{3} \cdot e^{3 x}+C {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 2.e x + C.e -2x
solution: The given differential equation is:- {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+2 y=6 e^{x}{/tex} This is of the form {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex} Where P = 2 and Q = 6.e x Thus the given differential equation is linear differential equation Now, {tex}I F=e^{\int P dx}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I F=e^{\int 2 \, d x} \Rightarrow I F=e^{2 x}{/tex} Solution is {tex}y \cdot(I F)=\int(I F) Q \, d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot e^{2 x}=\int e^{2 x} \cdot 6 \cdot e^{x} d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot e^{2 x}=6 \int e^{3 x} d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot e^{2 x}=\frac{6}{3} \cdot e^{3 x}+C {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 2.e x + C.e -2x
solution_html:
The given differential equation is:- {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+2 y=6 e^{x}{/tex}
This is of the form {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex}
Where P = 2 and Q = 6.ex
Thus the given differential equation is linear differential equation
Now, {tex}I F=e^{\int P dx}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I F=e^{\int 2 \, d x} \Rightarrow I F=e^{2 x}{/tex}
Solution is  {tex}y \cdot(I F)=\int(I F) Q \, d x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot e^{2 x}=\int e^{2 x} \cdot 6 \cdot e^{x} d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot e^{2 x}=6 \int e^{3 x} d x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot e^{2 x}=\frac{6}{3} \cdot e^{3 x}+C {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 2.ex + C.e-2x

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+8 y=5 e^{-3 x}{/tex}
question_text: Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+8 y=5 e^{-3 x}{/tex}
explanation: The given differential equation is: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+8 y=5 \cdot e^{-3 x} {/tex} This is of the form {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex} Where P = 8 and Q = e -3x Thus the given differential equation is linear Now, {tex}I F=e^{\int 8 \, dx}=e^{8 x}{/tex} {tex}\therefore (I F) \cdot y=\int(I F) Q \, d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow e^{3 x} \cdot y=\int e^{8 x} \cdot e^{-3 x} d x+C \Rightarrow e^{3 x} \cdot y=\int e^{5 x} d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow e^{\mathrm{8} x} \cdot y=\frac{1}{5} \cdot e^{5 x}+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y=\frac{e^{-3 x}}{5}+C \cdot e^{-8 x}{/tex}
solution: The given differential equation is: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+8 y=5 \cdot e^{-3 x} {/tex} This is of the form {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex} Where P = 8 and Q = e -3x Thus the given differential equation is linear Now, {tex}I F=e^{\int 8 \, dx}=e^{8 x}{/tex} {tex}\therefore (I F) \cdot y=\int(I F) Q \, d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow e^{3 x} \cdot y=\int e^{8 x} \cdot e^{-3 x} d x+C \Rightarrow e^{3 x} \cdot y=\int e^{5 x} d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow e^{\mathrm{8} x} \cdot y=\frac{1}{5} \cdot e^{5 x}+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y=\frac{e^{-3 x}}{5}+C \cdot e^{-8 x}{/tex}
solution_html:
The given differential equation is: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+8 y=5 \cdot e^{-3 x} {/tex}
This is of the form {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex}
Where P = 8 and Q = e-3x
Thus the given differential equation is linear
Now,  {tex}I F=e^{\int 8 \, dx}=e^{8 x}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore (I F) \cdot y=\int(I F) Q \, d x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow e^{3 x} \cdot y=\int e^{8 x} \cdot e^{-3 x} d x+C \Rightarrow e^{3 x} \cdot y=\int e^{5 x} d x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow e^{\mathrm{8} x} \cdot y=\frac{1}{5} \cdot e^{5 x}+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow y=\frac{e^{-3 x}}{5}+C \cdot e^{-8 x}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Solve the differential equation: (x2 + 3xy + y2) dx - x2 dy = 0
question_text: Solve the differential equation: (x 2 + 3xy + y 2 ) dx - x 2 dy = 0
explanation: The given differential equation is, (x 2 + 3xy + y 2 ) dx - x 2 dy = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}+3 x y+y^{2}}{x^{2}}{/tex} This is a homogeneous differential equation Putting y = vx and {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} , we get {tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}+3 v x^{2}+v^{2} x^{2}}{x^{2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = 1 + 3v + v 2 - v {tex}\Rightarrow x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = 1 + v 2 + 2v {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{1+v^{2}+2 v} d v=\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} Integrating both sides, we get {tex}\int \frac{1}{1+v^{2}+2 v} d v=\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{(1+v)^{2}} d v=\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow-\frac{1}{(1+v)}{/tex} = log |x| + C {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log |x| + {tex}\frac{1}{(1+v)}{/tex} = -C Putting v = {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex} , we get {tex}\therefore{/tex} log |x| + {tex}\frac{x}{(x+y)}{/tex} = C 1 where, C 1 = -C Hence, log |x| + {tex}\frac{x}{(x+y)}{/tex} = C 1 is the required solution.
solution: The given differential equation is, (x 2 + 3xy + y 2 ) dx - x 2 dy = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}+3 x y+y^{2}}{x^{2}}{/tex} This is a homogeneous differential equation Putting y = vx and {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} , we get {tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}+3 v x^{2}+v^{2} x^{2}}{x^{2}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = 1 + 3v + v 2 - v {tex}\Rightarrow x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = 1 + v 2 + 2v {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{1+v^{2}+2 v} d v=\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} Integrating both sides, we get {tex}\int \frac{1}{1+v^{2}+2 v} d v=\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{(1+v)^{2}} d v=\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow-\frac{1}{(1+v)}{/tex} = log |x| + C {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log |x| + {tex}\frac{1}{(1+v)}{/tex} = -C Putting v = {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex} , we get {tex}\therefore{/tex} log |x| + {tex}\frac{x}{(x+y)}{/tex} = C 1 where, C 1 = -C Hence, log |x| + {tex}\frac{x}{(x+y)}{/tex} = C 1 is the required solution.
solution_html:
The given differential equation is,

 (x2 + 3xy + y2) dx - x2 dy = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}+3 x y+y^{2}}{x^{2}}{/tex}
This is a homogeneous differential equation
Putting y = vx and {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex}, we get
{tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}+3 v x^{2}+v^{2} x^{2}}{x^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = 1 + 3v + v2 - v
{tex}\Rightarrow x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} = 1 + v2 + 2v
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{1+v^{2}+2 v} d v=\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex}
Integrating both sides, we get
{tex}\int \frac{1}{1+v^{2}+2 v} d v=\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{1}{(1+v)^{2}} d v=\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow-\frac{1}{(1+v)}{/tex} = log |x| + C
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log |x| + {tex}\frac{1}{(1+v)}{/tex} = -C
Putting v = {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex}, we get
{tex}\therefore{/tex} log |x| + {tex}\frac{x}{(x+y)}{/tex} = C1
where, C1 = -C
Hence, log |x| + {tex}\frac{x}{(x+y)}{/tex} = C1 is the required solution.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Verify that y = e{tex}^x{/tex} cos bx is a solution of the differential equation {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}-2 \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} + 2y = 0.
question_text: Verify that y = e {tex}^x{/tex} cos bx is a solution of the differential equation {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}-2 \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} + 2y = 0.
explanation: The given functional relation is given by, y = e x cos x ...(i) Differentiating both sides w.r.t x we have {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x} \frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (cos bx) + cos bx {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (e x ) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = -e x sin bx + e x cos x = e x [-sin bx + cos x] ...(ii) Differentiating both sides w.r.t x we get {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=-\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (e x sin x) + {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (e x cos x) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} = - {-e x {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (sin ) + sin x {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} e x } + e x {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (cos x) + cos x {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} e x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} = -e x cos x - e x sin x - e x sin x + e x cos x = e x {-cos x - 2 sin x + cos x} ...(iii) L.H.S. {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}-2 \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} + 2y = 0 = e x [-cos x - 2 sin x + cos x] -e x [-sin x + cos x] + 2e x {tex}\cdot{/tex} cos bx = e x [-cos x - 2 sin bx + cos x + 2 sin x - 2 cos x + 2 cos x] = e x {tex}\cdot{/tex} 0 = 0 = R.H.S. Hence given equation is a solution of given differential equation.
solution: The given functional relation is given by, y = e x cos x ...(i) Differentiating both sides w.r.t x we have {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x} \frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (cos bx) + cos bx {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (e x ) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = -e x sin bx + e x cos x = e x [-sin bx + cos x] ...(ii) Differentiating both sides w.r.t x we get {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=-\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (e x sin x) + {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (e x cos x) {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} = - {-e x {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (sin ) + sin x {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} e x } + e x {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (cos x) + cos x {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} e x {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} = -e x cos x - e x sin x - e x sin x + e x cos x = e x {-cos x - 2 sin x + cos x} ...(iii) L.H.S. {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}-2 \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} + 2y = 0 = e x [-cos x - 2 sin x + cos x] -e x [-sin x + cos x] + 2e x {tex}\cdot{/tex} cos bx = e x [-cos x - 2 sin bx + cos x + 2 sin x - 2 cos x + 2 cos x] = e x {tex}\cdot{/tex} 0 = 0 = R.H.S. Hence given equation is a solution of given differential equation.
solution_html:
The given functional relation is given by,

y = ex cos x ...(i)
Differentiating both sides w.r.t x we have {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x} \frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (cos bx) + cos bx {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (ex)
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = -ex sin bx + excos x = ex [-sin bx + cos x] ...(ii)
Differentiating both sides w.r.t x we get {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}=-\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (ex sin x) + {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (ex cos x)
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} = - {-ex {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (sin ) + sin x {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} ex} + ex {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} (cos x) + cos x {tex}\frac{d}{d x}{/tex} ex
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} = -ex cos x - ex sin x - ex sin x + ex cos x
= ex {-cos x - 2 sin x + cos x} ...(iii)
L.H.S. {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}-2 \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} + 2y = 0
= ex [-cos x - 2 sin x + cos x] -ex [-sin x + cos x] + 2ex {tex}\cdot{/tex} cos bx
= ex [-cos x - 2 sin bx + cos x + 2 sin x - 2 cos x + 2 cos x] = ex {tex}\cdot{/tex} 0 = 0 = R.H.S.
Hence given equation is a solution of given differential equation.

question_html: Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}-y=2 x^{3}{/tex}
question_text: Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}-y=2 x^{3}{/tex}
explanation: Given {tex}x \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}-y=2 x^{3}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}-\frac{1}{x} y=2 x^{2}{/tex} This is of the form {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex} Where {tex}P=-\frac{1}{x}{/tex} and Q = 2x 2 Now, {tex}I F=e^{\int P \, d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I F=e^{\int\left(\frac{-1}{x}\right) k}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I F=e^{-\ln x} \Rightarrow I, F=\frac{1}{x}{/tex} Therefore the solution is {tex}y(I-E)=\int(I-F) Q \, d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot \frac{1}{x}=\int \frac{1}{x}\left(2 x^{2}\right) d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{y}{x}=\int 2 x \, d x+C \Rightarrow \frac{y}{x}=x^{2}+C {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = x 3 + Cx.
solution: Given {tex}x \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}-y=2 x^{3}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}-\frac{1}{x} y=2 x^{2}{/tex} This is of the form {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex} Where {tex}P=-\frac{1}{x}{/tex} and Q = 2x 2 Now, {tex}I F=e^{\int P \, d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I F=e^{\int\left(\frac{-1}{x}\right) k}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I F=e^{-\ln x} \Rightarrow I, F=\frac{1}{x}{/tex} Therefore the solution is {tex}y(I-E)=\int(I-F) Q \, d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot \frac{1}{x}=\int \frac{1}{x}\left(2 x^{2}\right) d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{y}{x}=\int 2 x \, d x+C \Rightarrow \frac{y}{x}=x^{2}+C {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = x 3 + Cx.
solution_html:
Given {tex}x \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}-y=2 x^{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}-\frac{1}{x} y=2 x^{2}{/tex}
This is of the form {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex}
Where {tex}P=-\frac{1}{x}{/tex} and Q = 2x2
Now, {tex}I F=e^{\int P \, d x}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I F=e^{\int\left(\frac{-1}{x}\right) k}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I F=e^{-\ln x} \Rightarrow I, F=\frac{1}{x}{/tex}
Therefore the solution is {tex}y(I-E)=\int(I-F) Q \, d x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot \frac{1}{x}=\int \frac{1}{x}\left(2 x^{2}\right) d x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{y}{x}=\int 2 x \, d x+C \Rightarrow \frac{y}{x}=x^{2}+C {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = x3 + Cx.

question_html: Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\left(1-x^{2}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}+x y=a x{/tex}
question_text: Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\left(1-x^{2}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}+x y=a x{/tex}
explanation: The given differential equation is, {tex}\left(1-x^{2}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}+x y=a x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}+\frac{x}{1-x^{2}} \cdot y=\frac{a x}{1-x^{2}}{/tex} This is of the form {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex} where {tex}P=\frac{x}{1-x^{2}}{/tex} and {tex}Q=\frac{a x}{1-x^{2}}{/tex} This the given differential equation is linear Now, {tex}I F=e^{\int P dx}{/tex} {tex}=e^{\int \frac{x dx}{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} {tex}=e^{\frac{-1}{2}\int \frac{-2x }{1-x^{2}}dx} =e^{-\frac{1}{2} \log \left(1-x^{2}\right)}=\left(1-x^{2}\right)^{\frac{-1}{2}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} Solution is {tex}(I F) \cdot y=\int(I F) Q \, d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} \cdot y=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2 }} \cdot \frac{a x}{1-x^{2}} d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{y}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}=\int \frac{a x}{\left(1-x^{2}\right)^{3 / 2}} d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{y}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}=-\frac{a}{2} \int\frac{-2 x}{\left(1-x^{2}\right)^{3 / 2}} d x+C{/tex} ....(i) Now, {tex}I=\int \frac{-2 x}{\left(1-x^{2}\right)^{3 / 2}}dx{/tex} Let t = 1 - x 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dt = -2x dx {tex}\therefore I=\int \frac{d t}{t^{3 / 2}} \ \Rightarrow I=\int t^{-3 / 2} d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{t^{\frac{-1}{2}}}{-\frac{1}{2}} \Rightarrow I=-2 \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{t}}=\frac{-2}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{y}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}=\frac{-a}{2} \times \frac{-2}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y=a+C \sqrt{1-x^{2}}{/tex}
solution: The given differential equation is, {tex}\left(1-x^{2}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}+x y=a x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}+\frac{x}{1-x^{2}} \cdot y=\frac{a x}{1-x^{2}}{/tex} This is of the form {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex} where {tex}P=\frac{x}{1-x^{2}}{/tex} and {tex}Q=\frac{a x}{1-x^{2}}{/tex} This the given differential equation is linear Now, {tex}I F=e^{\int P dx}{/tex} {tex}=e^{\int \frac{x dx}{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} {tex}=e^{\frac{-1}{2}\int \frac{-2x }{1-x^{2}}dx} =e^{-\frac{1}{2} \log \left(1-x^{2}\right)}=\left(1-x^{2}\right)^{\frac{-1}{2}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} Solution is {tex}(I F) \cdot y=\int(I F) Q \, d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} \cdot y=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2 }} \cdot \frac{a x}{1-x^{2}} d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{y}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}=\int \frac{a x}{\left(1-x^{2}\right)^{3 / 2}} d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{y}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}=-\frac{a}{2} \int\frac{-2 x}{\left(1-x^{2}\right)^{3 / 2}} d x+C{/tex} ....(i) Now, {tex}I=\int \frac{-2 x}{\left(1-x^{2}\right)^{3 / 2}}dx{/tex} Let t = 1 - x 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dt = -2x dx {tex}\therefore I=\int \frac{d t}{t^{3 / 2}} \ \Rightarrow I=\int t^{-3 / 2} d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{t^{\frac{-1}{2}}}{-\frac{1}{2}} \Rightarrow I=-2 \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{t}}=\frac{-2}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \frac{y}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}=\frac{-a}{2} \times \frac{-2}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y=a+C \sqrt{1-x^{2}}{/tex}
solution_html:
The given differential equation is,
{tex}\left(1-x^{2}\right) \frac{d y}{d x}+x y=a x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}+\frac{x}{1-x^{2}} \cdot y=\frac{a x}{1-x^{2}}{/tex}
This is of the form {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex}
where {tex}P=\frac{x}{1-x^{2}}{/tex} and {tex}Q=\frac{a x}{1-x^{2}}{/tex}
This the given differential equation is linear
Now, {tex}I F=e^{\int P dx}{/tex}
{tex}=e^{\int \frac{x dx}{1-x^{2}}}{/tex}
{tex}=e^{\frac{-1}{2}\int \frac{-2x }{1-x^{2}}dx} =e^{-\frac{1}{2} \log \left(1-x^{2}\right)}=\left(1-x^{2}\right)^{\frac{-1}{2}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex}
Solution is
{tex}(I F) \cdot y=\int(I F) Q \, d x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}} \cdot y=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2 }} \cdot \frac{a x}{1-x^{2}} d x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{y}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}=\int \frac{a x}{\left(1-x^{2}\right)^{3 / 2}} d x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{y}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}=-\frac{a}{2} \int\frac{-2 x}{\left(1-x^{2}\right)^{3 / 2}} d x+C{/tex} ....(i)
Now, {tex}I=\int \frac{-2 x}{\left(1-x^{2}\right)^{3 / 2}}dx{/tex}
Let t = 1 - x2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dt = -2x dx
{tex}\therefore I=\int \frac{d t}{t^{3 / 2}} \ \Rightarrow I=\int t^{-3 / 2} d t{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow I=\frac{t^{\frac{-1}{2}}}{-\frac{1}{2}} \Rightarrow I=-2 \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{t}}=\frac{-2}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \frac{y}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}=\frac{-a}{2} \times \frac{-2}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow y=a+C \sqrt{1-x^{2}}{/tex}

question_html: Show that the differential equation (x2 + xy) dy = (x2 + y2)dx is homogeneous and solve it.
question_text: Show that the differential equation (x 2 + xy) dy = (x 2 + y 2 )dx is homogeneous and solve it.
explanation: We can rewrite the given differential equation as, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}+y^{2}}{x^{2}+x y}{/tex} On dividing the Nr and Dr of RHS of (i) by x 2 , we get {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\left\{1+\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)^{2}\right\}}{\left\{1+\frac{y}{x}\right\}}=f\left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous. Put y = vx and {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} in (i), we get {tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}+v^{2} x^{2}}{x^{2}+v x^{2}}=\frac{1+v^{2}}{1+v}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{\left(1+v^{2}\right)}{(1+v)}-v=\frac{1+v^{2}-v-v^{2}}{(1+v)}=\frac{(1-v)}{(1+v)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{(1+v)}{(1-v)} d v=\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{(1+v)}{(1-v)} d v=\int \frac{1}{x} dx{/tex} [on integrating both sides] {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{| 2-(1-v)\}}{(1-v)} d v=\int \frac{1}{x} dx{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int\left\{\frac{2}{(1-v)}-1\right\} d v=\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2 log |1 - v| -v = log |x| + log |C| where C is an arbitrary constant {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log |x| + log |C| + 2 log |1 - v| = -v {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log |Cx (1 - v) 2 | = -v {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |C x(1-v) 2 | = e -v {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |Cx {tex}(1-\frac{y}{x})^2{/tex} | = e -y/x [ {tex}\because{/tex} v = {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex} ] {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} C|(x - y) 2 = |x| e -y/x , which is the required solution.
solution: We can rewrite the given differential equation as, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}+y^{2}}{x^{2}+x y}{/tex} On dividing the Nr and Dr of RHS of (i) by x 2 , we get {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\left\{1+\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)^{2}\right\}}{\left\{1+\frac{y}{x}\right\}}=f\left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex} Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous. Put y = vx and {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex} in (i), we get {tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}+v^{2} x^{2}}{x^{2}+v x^{2}}=\frac{1+v^{2}}{1+v}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{\left(1+v^{2}\right)}{(1+v)}-v=\frac{1+v^{2}-v-v^{2}}{(1+v)}=\frac{(1-v)}{(1+v)}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{(1+v)}{(1-v)} d v=\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{(1+v)}{(1-v)} d v=\int \frac{1}{x} dx{/tex} [on integrating both sides] {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{| 2-(1-v)\}}{(1-v)} d v=\int \frac{1}{x} dx{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \int\left\{\frac{2}{(1-v)}-1\right\} d v=\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2 log |1 - v| -v = log |x| + log |C| where C is an arbitrary constant {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log |x| + log |C| + 2 log |1 - v| = -v {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log |Cx (1 - v) 2 | = -v {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |C x(1-v) 2 | = e -v {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |Cx {tex}(1-\frac{y}{x})^2{/tex} | = e -y/x [ {tex}\because{/tex} v = {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex} ] {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} C|(x - y) 2 = |x| e -y/x , which is the required solution.
solution_html:
We can rewrite the given differential equation as,
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}+y^{2}}{x^{2}+x y}{/tex}
On dividing the Nr and Dr of RHS of (i) by x2, we get
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{\left\{1+\left(\frac{y}{x}\right)^{2}\right\}}{\left\{1+\frac{y}{x}\right\}}=f\left(\frac{y}{x}\right){/tex}
Therefore, the given differential equation is homogeneous.
Put y = vx and {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=v+x \frac{d v}{d x}{/tex}in (i), we get
{tex}v+x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{x^{2}+v^{2} x^{2}}{x^{2}+v x^{2}}=\frac{1+v^{2}}{1+v}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad x \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{\left(1+v^{2}\right)}{(1+v)}-v=\frac{1+v^{2}-v-v^{2}}{(1+v)}=\frac{(1-v)}{(1+v)}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{(1+v)}{(1-v)} d v=\frac{1}{x} d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{(1+v)}{(1-v)} d v=\int \frac{1}{x} dx{/tex} [on integrating both sides]
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{| 2-(1-v)\}}{(1-v)} d v=\int \frac{1}{x} dx{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \int\left\{\frac{2}{(1-v)}-1\right\} d v=\int \frac{1}{x} d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2 log |1 - v| -v = log |x| + log |C| where C is an arbitrary constant
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log |x| + log |C| + 2 log |1 - v| = -v
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} log |Cx (1 - v)2| = -v
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |C x(1-v)2| = e-v
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |Cx  {tex}(1-\frac{y}{x})^2{/tex}| = e-y/x [{tex}\because{/tex} v = {tex}\frac{y}{x}{/tex}]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} C|(x - y)2 = |x| e-y/x, which is the required solution.

question_html: Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} + y cot x = x2 cot x + 2x
question_text: Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} + y cot x = x 2 cot x + 2x
explanation: The given differential equation is, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} + y cot x = x 2 cot x + 2x This is of the form {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex} , where P = cot x and Q = x 2 cot x + 2x Thus the given differential equation is linear Now, {tex}I F=e^{\int P dx} \Rightarrow I F=e^{\int \cot x \, d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I F=e^{log(sin x)} \Rightarrow I F=\sin x{/tex} {tex}\therefore (I F) y=\int(I F) Q \, d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow(\sin x) y-\int \sin x\left(x^{2} \cot x+2 x\right) d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sin x \cdot y=\int\left(x^{2} \cos x+2 x \cdot \sin x\right) d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sin x \cdot y=\int x^{2} \cos x d x+\int 2 x \cdot \sin x \, d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sin x \cdot y=x^{2} \int \cos x \, d x-\int\left\{\frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{2}\right) \int \cos x \, d x d x+\int 2 x \sin x \, d x+C\right.{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sin x \cdot y=x^{2} \sin x-\int 2 x \sin x \, d x+\int 2 x \cdot \sin x \, d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sin x . y = x 2 sin x + C {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = x 2 + C cosec x
solution: The given differential equation is, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} + y cot x = x 2 cot x + 2x This is of the form {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex} , where P = cot x and Q = x 2 cot x + 2x Thus the given differential equation is linear Now, {tex}I F=e^{\int P dx} \Rightarrow I F=e^{\int \cot x \, d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I F=e^{log(sin x)} \Rightarrow I F=\sin x{/tex} {tex}\therefore (I F) y=\int(I F) Q \, d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow(\sin x) y-\int \sin x\left(x^{2} \cot x+2 x\right) d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sin x \cdot y=\int\left(x^{2} \cos x+2 x \cdot \sin x\right) d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sin x \cdot y=\int x^{2} \cos x d x+\int 2 x \cdot \sin x \, d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sin x \cdot y=x^{2} \int \cos x \, d x-\int\left\{\frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{2}\right) \int \cos x \, d x d x+\int 2 x \sin x \, d x+C\right.{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \sin x \cdot y=x^{2} \sin x-\int 2 x \sin x \, d x+\int 2 x \cdot \sin x \, d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sin x . y = x 2 sin x + C {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = x 2 + C cosec x
solution_html:
The given differential equation is,

{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} + y cot x = x2 cot x + 2x
This is of the form {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex}, where P = cot x and Q = x2 cot x + 2x
Thus the given differential equation is linear
Now, {tex}I F=e^{\int P dx} \Rightarrow I F=e^{\int \cot x \, d x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow I F=e^{log(sin x)} \Rightarrow I F=\sin x{/tex}
{tex}\therefore (I F) y=\int(I F) Q \, d x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow(\sin x) y-\int \sin x\left(x^{2} \cot x+2 x\right) d x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \sin x \cdot y=\int\left(x^{2} \cos x+2 x \cdot \sin x\right) d x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \sin x \cdot y=\int x^{2} \cos x d x+\int 2 x \cdot \sin x \, d x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \sin x \cdot y=x^{2} \int \cos x \, d x-\int\left\{\frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{2}\right) \int \cos x \, d x d x+\int 2 x \sin x \, d x+C\right.{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \sin x \cdot y=x^{2} \sin x-\int 2 x \sin x \, d x+\int 2 x \cdot \sin x \, d x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sin x . y = x2 sin x + C
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = x2 + C cosec x

question_html: Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}2 x \frac{d y}{d x}+y=6 x^{3}{/tex}
question_text: Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}2 x \frac{d y}{d x}+y=6 x^{3}{/tex}
explanation: Given differential equation is {tex}2 x \frac{d y}{d x}+y=6 x^{3}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}+\frac{1}{2 x} \cdot y=3 x^{2}{/tex} This is of the form {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex} where {tex}P=\frac{1}{2 x}{/tex} and Q = 3x 2 Thus the differential equation is linear Now, {tex}IF=e^{\int P dx}{/tex} {tex}=e^{\int \frac{d x}{2 x}}=e^{\frac{1}{2} \log x}=x^{1 / 2}{/tex} There fore the solution is given by {tex}y(I F)=\int(I F) Q \, d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot x^{1 / 2}=\int x^{1 / 2} \cdot 3 x^{2} d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot x^{1 / 2}=3 \int x^{3 / 2} d x+C \Rightarrow y \cdot x^{1 / 2}=3 \cdot \frac{x}{7 / 2}+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot x^{1/ 2}=\frac{6}{7} x^{1 / 2}+C \Rightarrow y=\frac{6}{7} \cdot x^{3}+\frac{C}{\sqrt{x}}{/tex} This is the required solution of given differential equation.
solution: Given differential equation is {tex}2 x \frac{d y}{d x}+y=6 x^{3}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}+\frac{1}{2 x} \cdot y=3 x^{2}{/tex} This is of the form {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex} where {tex}P=\frac{1}{2 x}{/tex} and Q = 3x 2 Thus the differential equation is linear Now, {tex}IF=e^{\int P dx}{/tex} {tex}=e^{\int \frac{d x}{2 x}}=e^{\frac{1}{2} \log x}=x^{1 / 2}{/tex} There fore the solution is given by {tex}y(I F)=\int(I F) Q \, d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot x^{1 / 2}=\int x^{1 / 2} \cdot 3 x^{2} d x+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot x^{1 / 2}=3 \int x^{3 / 2} d x+C \Rightarrow y \cdot x^{1 / 2}=3 \cdot \frac{x}{7 / 2}+C{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot x^{1/ 2}=\frac{6}{7} x^{1 / 2}+C \Rightarrow y=\frac{6}{7} \cdot x^{3}+\frac{C}{\sqrt{x}}{/tex} This is the required solution of given differential equation.
solution_html:
Given differential equation is {tex}2 x \frac{d y}{d x}+y=6 x^{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}+\frac{1}{2 x} \cdot y=3 x^{2}{/tex}
This is of the form {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}+P y=Q{/tex} where {tex}P=\frac{1}{2 x}{/tex} and Q = 3x2
Thus the differential equation is linear
Now, {tex}IF=e^{\int P dx}{/tex}
{tex}=e^{\int \frac{d x}{2 x}}=e^{\frac{1}{2} \log x}=x^{1 / 2}{/tex}
There fore the solution is given by
{tex}y(I F)=\int(I F) Q \, d x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot x^{1 / 2}=\int x^{1 / 2} \cdot 3 x^{2} d x+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot x^{1 / 2}=3 \int x^{3 / 2} d x+C \Rightarrow y \cdot x^{1 / 2}=3 \cdot \frac{x}{7 / 2}+C{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow y \cdot x^{1/ 2}=\frac{6}{7} x^{1 / 2}+C \Rightarrow y=\frac{6}{7} \cdot x^{3}+\frac{C}{\sqrt{x}}{/tex}
This is the required solution of given differential equation.
